title: Challenging, but needs tweaking
text:
This is a brain-teasing game that requires you to look at drawings, words, numbers and letters and decipher them according to their placement, size and arrangement (among other things) to come up with phrases, names, proverbs, movies, songs and more.

While fairly addictive, each level has 60 puzzles, and you need to solve 50 of them before you can move on. Each puzzle has three hints, but you are limited to 19 hints, with a new hint popping up every 300 seconds or after five guesses.

My problems with this game: (Using a Kindle Fire)

1. The font used is hard to read, and doesn't fit properly in the available word window. Too many letters look the same leading to far too many uncorrected and unintentional typos.

2. The accepted solutions are inconsistent, with some answers being judged incorrect for capital letters and lack of punctuation, while others getting the nod while having just one correct key word. This is annoying.

3. You can't jump to a category of your choice, but have to go through them in the given order, getting at least 50 correct in each category. (I am on level 6, but would have preferred to choose the category myself)

4. The music has to go

Overall a reasonably entertaining brain game, but could use some improvement.

Amanda Richards, November 16, 2013

Dec 27 - Finished the game. Comments above still stand.
When you finish the last category you get a notification that you've unlocked another level, but there isn't another.

A little tweaking would make this a much better game

title: HDR made easy
text:
If you've ever wished you could create beautiful HDR images, this software can make your dreams come true, as it did for me. I learned about Photomatix Pro from [[ASIN:B0048EKIR4 Improve Your HDR Photography (Improve Your Photography)]]by Jim Harmer. The program is also available on-line at hdrsoft dot com, where you can get a free trial before buying.

This program is extremely easy to use, and once you've taken your three shots with auto-exposure bracketing, or more exposures at your choosing, you just click to upload them, and then follow the on screen choices. A tripod is certainly necessary for good clean HDR images, but this software is so flexible that it will take your hand-held (slightly off) images, and align them, also fixing any "ghosting" from moving trees or shaky images. You can also use single images and apply tone-mapping, which also gives dynamic images. Simple sliders allow you to tweak your images before saving them.

Your first tries will no doubt prove to be eye-openers, but keep in mind that the best images are the ones that are not "overdone" and totally fake looking. Professionals use this program, and given its simplicity and affordability, so can you.

Amanda Richards, January 15, 2012

title: Hasta la vista, McAfee
text:
John Connor: No, no, no, no. You gotta listen to the way people talk. You don't say "affirmative", or some #### like that. You say "no problemo". And if someone comes on to you with an attitude you say "eat me". And if you want to shine them on it's "hasta la vista, baby" (Terminator 2: Judgment Day)

1. The first thing I noticed about Norton was how fast it was installed - less than 2 minutes on my 3 year old computer running Vista, no doubt much faster on newer machines.

2. Activation after the installation was a breeze. I already had a Norton Account, so it easily processed the updates.

3. It is recommended that you update your virus definitions once the installation is complete to ensure that you are fully protected. I used LiveUpdate, which took some time given my limited bandwidth, and at the end the update of the definitions failed (although the other program updates were successful.) However, the summary page had a handy link which invoked One Click Support and the information there provided me with the instructions for an alternative way to get the required updates, a fairly painless process. People not hindered by lack of bandwidth shouldn't have any problems downloading the latest virus definitions and program updates.

4. Once installed, the interface is very simple to use and, unlike McAfee, you can easily see the date the latest virus definitions were uploaded.

5. It doesn't seem to have too much of an impact on computer resources, and like a good little snitch, it alerts you via pop-up when another program is using significant resources.

6. Tonight, for the first time since I installed the software (3 days ago), my computer seemed to hang up while shutting down. I don't know if this is a problem caused by Norton Internet Security as mentioned by another user, and I didn't do any troubleshooting to try to identify the source of the problem.

7. A pleasant surprise was the facebook scan app. which scans your (almost obligatory) facebook page for any malicious links/apps.

8. The firewall works quietly in the background with no pop up messages asking you to allow/block programmes from accessing the internet, and I'll assume that the anti-virus does its job, based on past experience with Norton.

Summary:

1. Tried for days to install McAfee
2. Gave up
3. Installed Norton instead
4. No problemo

title: Surfing in the green zone, shredding the "wares"
text:
This version of McAfee offers a whole bundle of features, including some cool stuff that isn't on previous versions. Installation is fast and easy, even though you have to remove your existing anti-virus software, and it doesn't cause application errors or slow down system performance. The single CD in the package can provide protection for up to three computers for one year, after which you need to shell out for the annual fee at the going rate. Installation also offers the option of installing from the CD provided and then updating from the web after installation is completed or downloading the application from the web and then installing.

It has a simple, user friendly appearance and a choice of two interfaces -basic and advanced. It should be noted that some of the options are only available to persons logged in as "administrators" and not to the other users on your computer with limited permissions. If you're the only user you can consider yourself promoted to Administrative level.

"So what does it DO?" enquiring minds want to know. Well, here goes.

1. It provides a firewall as your computer's first line of defense against identity theft, viruses, spyware, spam, hackers and other bad things that go "bump" in cyberspace. There's a "lockdown" feature that stops all internet access with one click.

2. It makes web-surfing safer by rating your search results, as well as the sites you visit, using a color code. Green = safe; Yellow = caution; Red = get the heck out of there NOW. (Works with Google, Yahoo! And MSN searches.)

3. Automatic updates and upgrades

4. Stops viruses in their tracks, as well as worms, Trojans and other slimy things, and then automatically cleans or quarantines the little boogers. The QuickScan feature patrols the vulnerable areas of your computer to check for threats.

5. Offers you a "stealth mode" that blocks incoming network connections so you can lurk on the internet without being detected by hackers.

6. Blocks spyware, adware, and other bad "wares" from stealing your log-ins and passwords, tracking your cookies, or filling your screen with pop-ups.

7. Keeps your computer uncluttered with a QuickClean system that takes out the trash, such as temporary files, cached files, lost file fragments, deleted e-mail messages and other undesirable junk.

8. It includes a "Shredder" for permanently deleting top secret documents such as love letters or romantic poems penned under the influence. To make it even cooler, there are three different shredding levels.

9. Automatically recognizes spam, phish and scams, fake virus messages and all that weird foreign language stuff, and stops it from filling your in-box

10. Provides different levels of blocking depending on the age or maturity level of your child. It has easy to understand settings like "young child", "child", "younger teen", "older teen" or "adult". Age appropriate searching makes sure that the safety filters of some popular search engines are enabled. It uses keyword filtering and even blocks offensive images.

11. Provides an automatic back-up feature which requires the installation of Microsoft.net Framework 1.1

12. Tactfully defers certain updates and alerts while you're watching movies or playing games in full-screen mode to prevent interruptions.

Recommended for PCs and small networks, families with internet-savvy kids and people who like to go into sites marked with 3 X's.

Amanda Richards, October 15, 2008

title: 'Cos hey - you never know
text:
If you have a home office or small business and need protection against the nasty bugs and sneaky worms that thrive in the cyberspace lottery, then McAfee Total Protection can help you make sure that your number never comes up.

The default settings provide very strong protection against viruses, Trojans, worms, malicious ActiveX controls and Java applets. The SecurityCenter has easy tabs which make it simple to review your computer's security status, check for updates, and fix potential security issues. Besides the VirusScan tab there's also Personal Firewall Plus, Privacy Service and SpamKiller.

McAfee automatically downloads updated virus definitions from the internet on a regular basis. Staying up to date will dramatically decrease any possibility that a virus will infect your computer. McAfee usually comes out with new virus definitions on a daily basis. A subscription is needed for this service, but this package includes licenses for three users. Techies at the office have also reported that McAfee uses less system resources than Symantec and also picks up viruses that Symantec might miss.

McAfee VirusScan offers a wide list of features. You can set VirusScan to scan your computer at a preprogrammed time. The program also scans for spyware and adware. The SpamKiller really knows its stuff, and also updates automatically.

With regard to installation, there are absolutely no problems when installing to a clean system (no previously installed Virus Suite.) However, when switching from Symantec to McAfee you may need to do some research online to get Symantec uninstalled.

Bottom line: Using McAfee improves your odds against the critters of cyberspace, so make sure you get it right the first time.

Amanda Richards, June 10, 2008

title: MS HomeCare for the Non-technical
text:
If you're reading this I'm guessing that you're not a techie. As far as I have been able to determine, no self-respecting techie will touch this programme with a ten foot pole, but it does have its uses as a self-updating cure-all for small home systems.

The thing is, Microsoft has enemies, and due to the dedication of this group, the company has developed its own security system to protect customers against viruses, Trojans, spyware, phishing and all the other bad stuff coming at you through the internet.

Live Onecare is actually a combination of lots of stuff that's already on your computer, plus a spyware detector and a firewall, all nicely set up in an idiot-proof interface. Basically, it's a simple way of protecting your computer and keeping junk off your hard drive, now in a self-cleaning and self-updating model.

Pros:
1. If you're not familiar with your computer's system tools, this programme makes things like Disk Defrag, Disk Clean-up and Backup easier to find.
2. Automatically updates once you're connected to the internet
3. Can be installed on three computers with one Windows Live ID
4. Works as your own personal IT Department, fixing some problems before you know that they're there.

Cons:
1. Seeing that this is a Microsoft programme that repackages and organizes existing Microsoft software to help Microsoft customers with Microsoft problems, it should be available as a free download for those who need it. The annual charge is nothing short of super-highway robbery.
2. Existing anti-virus and firewall programmes have to be removed before installation.
3. It doesn't pick up new viruses as fast as other programmes like McAfee and Symantec
4. It doesn't protect network servers, if you happen to have one at home, that is.
5. No anti-spam protection
6. The plastic packaging is like a firewall, resisting entry by intruders like yourself.

Conclusion:
1. If you're a techie you don't need this

2. If you're not a techie:
a. Ask someone to show you the Accessories - System tools menu
b. Keep your existing anti-virus software up to date
c. Don't download files attached to e-mails from untrustworthy sources (strangers aren't sending you greeting cards, and Jessica Biel probably isn't really nekkid either)
d. Stop frequenting those adult sites
e. Do all of the above and you don't need this.

3. If you're really not into computers and you just use yours for e-mail, writing letters, and shopping at Amazon, you might find this programme useful as a one-stop hassle-free service center.

Amanda Richards, March 19, 2008

title: The best unknown software ever made
text:
As a small part of my job, I design flyers, formal invitations, posters, business documents, advertisements, and bulletins, and also update the company website. On my own limited time, I make greeting cards, invitations, banners and all sorts of other stuff.
I have used Microsoft Publisher from the inception, and it is the easiest, most idiot proof programme available for all of the above and more. Beginners can use the wizards and templates, which can be modified as you get more familiar with the programme. I am a self taught Publisher user, and if I can do it, anyone can.
Unfortunately, nobody in the professional advertising world and world-wide web uses or has any form of Publisher, so my best advertising creations have to be copied and saved in Microsoft Word, and sent to the advertising agencies to set up in Corel Draw. (I tried teaching myself Corel Draw, but it's not as easy as Publisher, and I gave up)
No website host works with Publisher either, so I have had to begin learning Dreamweaver, which is more complicated and only gives you a tiny space to work in, unlike the full page to which I am accustomed.
The only other drawback I have found is that the clipart collection is stored on-line, so you have to log on to the internet to get clipart pictures, and if you have a slow connection, you're there all day waiting for pictures to come up. They should have some basic clips readily available for simple projects, as in earlier versions.
For people who don't have to interface with the media and web site hosts, this programme is ideal for all your desktop publishing requirements.

title: Complete functionality
text: I have this on my laptop and it connects with the Microsoft 365 seamlessly. I can do more detailed documents on original Word than on the 365 app version, but the 365 app pulls it up correctly. The Excel spreadsheets work as well. The key here is the ability to access my files in the office, in the truck, or on a job site without obnoxious drama.

title: Makes working in the field a breeze
text:
I've recently taken over as director of a major city department and that means working in the office, around town, out in the industrial park and on the road sometimes. Just because I'm not in my physical office, doesn't mean documents don't get written, edited or printed for someone else. I could not be more pleased with Microsoft 365 for my needs.

I've used it at a City Council meeting on my Samsun Note phone, at a construction site on my tablet and of course on my laptop in various situations. Every document autosaves and can be accessed from my other devices as needed with zero drama. You're just carrying your entire workspace around in the cloud. And it actually works perfectly.

title: Lots of interesting options
text: There's more variety of shows on here than I expected. The picture quality is very good as well. My only issue is the clunky layout - it's difficult to sort out the genres and find something to watch.

title: Annual subscription opens everything
text:
We dumped Disney plus for Paramount Plus and haven't looked back. We stream exclusively both at home and in our RV. We have the usual services.... HULU, Prime, Netflix, Peacock, Tubi and Paramount Plus with no regrets.

Paramount is best enjoyed if you're willing to commit to the annual subscription. It has a nice variety of old sitcoms, movies, documentaries from The Smithsonian channel and more. I find I watch it more than Netflix actually. The app works fine on our Fire Tablets and our smart tv.

title: Fast with nice picture quality
text:
We dumped cable and satellite four years ago and never looked back. Hulu has been part of our streaming selection from day one on the three main formats - Roku, Fire and Chromecast.

This app on a Fire Tablet is working perfectly and just like it does on our Roku TV. Our account info - saved shows, lists, profiles etc - ported fine between this app and the Roku version.

title: Easy to follow along and use every day.
text: I've been using Rosetta Stone for Spanish and I have seen some real improvement in my ability. The program is pretty straight forward and simple to do. You connect words and phrases with images rather than just rote memorization. It's interactive and you can repeat any section as needed until you feel ready to move forward.

title: Surprising selection of good stuff. Works fine with Firestick and Roku
text: I tried Pluto on a whim. I'm actually kind of amazed at the quality and selection of programming here. It's a nice mix of news, tv shows and older movies. In a week, I haven't gone to Pluto and been unable to find something to watch. It works fine on both our Firestick and our Roku device. This is a solid addition to our streaming options since we cut that cable cord.

title: This app is the work around to watch YouTube on the Fire Stick
text: I'm using this app on my Fire Stick specifically to watch YouTube. Since you can't get the regular YouTube app through Amazon, this is the work around. It works fine and I haven't experienced any stuttering, freezing or other problems.

title: Full cast books sound great via surround sound. App syncs perfectly so you pick up in the right spot
text:
I've been a die hard Audible fan for 7 years now and I use the app on my phone to stream to the Ford Sync system in my truck and with headphones, on my tablet and through Alexa every day. Having it on my TV seemed silly at first, but it's kind of amazing to hear books through the Bose surround sound. Especially the Audible full cast productions like Alien.

The Fire TV app is working perfectly via my Fire Stick. It shows my current library of books and suggestions for new books exactly as it does on my phone. The books sync exactly the same as with the app on any other device so you can pick up exactly where you left off. I'm quite pleased.

title: Changing my childish bad Spanglish to real Spanish. Amazing!
text:
Like most people, I've seen the ads for Rosetta Stone over the years and wondered if it was actually as good as they claimed or just another overhyped way to get our hard earned cash. I have to say that I am seriously amazed and impressed with this system after two weeks. I also wish I had done this long ago.

I'm a native Texan and as such I've always spoken a smattering of Spanish and the local patois of "Spanglish" you hear throughout our border communities. However, it's become apparent that having a better grasp of full Spanish is more necessary than not for many reasons, but especially after a guy drove into my parked, new truck with no driver's license, no car insurance and zero ability to speak English. So I've been using the Rosetta Stone system most evenings and it's not only working, it's far easier than expected and I am actually having a good time with it.

Each lesson is broken down into shirt sessions based on vocabulary, grammar, and pronounciation, using visual , written and audio aids. I find that I am grasping the new words, correct pronounciation and gender usage much more quickly this way than I ever have before with old school books or person to person instructions. Most importantly, I had to skip my nightly sessions for four nights and last night when I was able to start again, I actually remembered everything I had previously done with Rosetta Stone - that tells me this is working. I also had a short, simple conversation with a friend in regular Spanish this morning and he said my Spanglish was reduced, my real Spanish was more obvious and my grammar was noticably improved. (In other words, I sound less like a moron and more like a reasonable adult in his language) Yay!! I'm very happy with the progress and I will absolutely continue to use this system and recommend it to anyone else needing to pick up a second language.

title: Works perfectly with Fire Stick and Roku. Live channels, DVR and streaming libraries are fast
text:
Three months ago, we cut the cable cord completely. We bought a super high speed internet connection and went to totally streaming our tv service. I sampled all the services and we chose HULU LIVE with limited commercials as our primary service for several reasons.

The HULU LIVE gives us local channels, national channels live and all the streaming libraries and a cloud DVR service with about half the commercials for just forty three dollars a month. The app works well with both our Fire Sticks and the Roku Express. If you're using a Fire Stick, you can link it to Alexa and use verbal commands - very nice.

You can also use Hulu paid service to activate the apps for other national channels like Bravo, HGTV, etc if you want those streaming options on separate devices like a tablet or laptop. We also have Prime, Netflix, and free DirectTV Now streaming from our cellular provider, but we tend to use Hulu the most.

title: Not an actual guide to new shows available
text: Meh. This is just multiple one to three minutes videos talking about random shows or channels available on the Fire TV and Fire Sticks. I was thinking it was something like a guide to the latest or upcoming shows. It's not something we will use - mostly a gimmick.

title: Must activate with a paid streaming or cable service.
text:
We cut the cable cord once and for all in September and it's been a bit of a challenge to navigate our way through the streaming maze. In the long run, it's totally been worth it. The first thing you must realize is that none of these apps actually work unless you have some sort of paid service provider. You can only watch the shows after you've activated the app through a cable company, Hulu Live, Sling or DirectTV Now. So these premium channel apps are not truly free or accessible from scratch.

A&E was one of my must have channels and this app is useful for lots of their standard shows - complete episodes from all the seasons. Live PD can't be viewed live with the app though. That requires an actual live TV service like Hulu Live or DirectTV Now. Once you've activated the service, the app works fine on both the Fire stick and Roku Express and we can watch on a tablet with either WiFi or a simple cellular data connection.

title: Can activate with HULU Live. Still has commercials
text: Like all the different tv channel apps, you must already have a paid subscription to either call, satellite or streaming tv provider. You cannot watch any of the HGTV shows without paying someone for the privilege. Fortunately, this works with activation through HULU LIVE. There are still commercials in the middle of the shows, but significantly fewer than on live cable TV.

title: Requires activation with paid cable service
text: I'm pleased to see several fun shows available on this app for on demand viewing. I've installed it on both our Fire Stick and our Roku Express and it works well on both platforms. This is another app that has to be verified/activated through a paid cable or satellite TV service in order to actually watch shows.

title: Most of the app offerings are just old seasons - not the most current episodes
text:
I was surprised to realize that in order to watch anything on Bravo Now, you must activate the app by validating your have a paid satellite or cable subscription service. This is true of most of the popular stations' apps unfortunately. True streaming still requires paying one of the satellite or cable services for the right to watch TV. (I find this maddening)

Bravo Now works with the DirectTV Now streaming service and works fine on both our Roku Express and our Fire Sticks. However, it's not live or even most recent episodes of the most popular shows. This is primarily the on demand type of service for their shows with past seasons.

title: Not compatible with Direct TV NOW streaming service
text: I love this channel and dropping the overpriced cable from Suddenlink for streaming presents a problem. We have Direct TV Now streaming and ID Go can only be activated via cable or full Direct TV via satellite - not streaming. These tv companies need to get on the band wagon with the future....excessively expensive wired cable connections are the dinosaurs of television viewing.

title: Streams via Fire Stick with no delay or excess buffering
text: I'm using this app through my Fire Stick. It downloaded fast, zipped right to SHOWTIME for my login info and I was watching Twin Peaks in a couple of short clicks. The picture is clear and no buffering  during the show. (We have cable Internet for streaming) The app works as it should.

title: Free for one hour then you have to buy the app. Architectural and furnishing items are additional in app purchases
text: Not truly free - you get an hour to test it and then they want you to pay money. The interface is clunky on a tablet unless you use a stylus. Furthermore, you have to pay for most of the furniture and architectural items as you design a room layout - most of the furniture and things like French doors are in app purchases. I deleted the app simply because I don't like that bait and switch game.

title: Absolutely addictive. Plays smoothly on two Fire Tablets and a LG V10 phone
text: I am utterly and hopelessly addicted to this game. It's seductive in its seeming simplicity - just flip all the pieces over to your color and win. Except that it isn't quite as simple as it looks and you have to actually think a few a moves ahead. Each level gets more difficult and you find yourself as happy to tie the computer as beat it.  I'm playing this on two different Fire tablets and my LG V10 phone with no problems.

title: Installed on 2 laptops in minutes. Easy to use immediately - lots of extras included. See sample letter photo.
text:
I've used both WordPerfect and Microsoft Office over the years and liked them both. To be honest I don't really see much difference in the two for basic performance and ease of use for the basic home user or in my case - very tiny small agricultural business owner and occasional op/ed guest columnist for our local paper. The thing that wins me over to Corel's WordPerfect Office software this go round is quite simply the economics of it. For a much smaller one time price, you can purchase this package with a license for three computers and you are done. You own it and you can use it as long as you need to and your computers survive. Microsoft Office has gone to the rent-a-word program and I am not ok with that.

So installing this on two laptops was about as straight forward as it can be. Both my laptops are Windows 10 machines and one is brand new with almost nothing on it, the other has been use for almost a year. The single disc booted up immediately and did its thing with minimal input from me on both machines. You do need the serial number on the back of the CD envelope of course and to make some install choices about which components you want - like the Aftershot photo editing, the Lightening notepad, the extra font pack for the WordPerfect and a few others. Within 10 minutes I was up and creating a document and editing some photos. Could NOT be simpler.

The Aftershot photo stuff has way more stuff on it than I understand or will ever use to be honest. But for basic color adjustment and light issues on a few sample pictures it was really helpful last night. This morning I was using the WordPerfect for a letter to Starbucks to compliment two of their employees for making my early mornings so much more pleasant and working on an upcoming piece for the newspaper. The documents both saved easily in PDF and Docx formats and both printed with no drama. I'm including a photo of a sample formal letter from the included base Corel template with a piece of clip art that is also included in the software. (For some reason Amazon just insists on turning it sideways. I've uploaded it three times and rotated it differently each time, sorry) There are hundreds of fonts to choose from as well that are already included with no additional downloads required.

Bottom line for me is that this is a very nice word processor and the photo editor is full of goodies. You gets lots of bang for your one time purchase buck. Easy to install and easy to use.

title: Easily set to back up on WiFi only. Works great on LG V10 phone.
text: Having recently switched from my Fire Phone to a LG V10 I lost the immediate, automatic back up of my photos to my Amazon cloud. This app fixes that problem simply. It just works and you can set it to upload only on a WiFi connection if you don't have an unlimited data plan. Plus my photos are instantly available on my Fire Tablet after they upload from my phone. My only complaint is the fact that you still can't sort your photos easily into different categories.

title: Works well on the Fire 8" tablet. Sliding tile style rather than a jigsaw, free daily puzzle
text: I am surprised by how much I like this little puzzle game. I haven't purchased any of the puzzles, just doing the daily free puzzle has been enough for me so far. This isn't a traditional jigsaw style, but a shuffle and slide tile type of puzzle. It works perfectly on my Fire tablet and I find that the ten minuets or so it takes to finish the daily puzzle is enough to clear my brain for the rest of the afternoon's work.

title: Pretty photographs converted to jigsaws. You can vary the number of pieces & difficulty
text: Fun time killer for my Fire tablet. The free puzzles are very pretty, high quality photographs of famous paintings, gorgeous landscapes and interesting architecture. You can choose how many puzzle pieces you want thus changing the difficulty and length of time each one takes. I have experienced a bit of lag now and then if I do three or more in a row, but as a rule, the game runs smoothly and correctly.

title: Works perfectly for viewing & printing documents from my phone & tablet
text: I primarily use this to read and print email documents and files that I have to access on the run from my phone or tablet. It works perfectly for my purposes and is compatible with my Print Hand app for wireless printing from my Fire Phone and my Fire HDX tablet. After a year, I have not seen a need to purchase a different or fancier version.

title: Works exactly as it should with my Fire products.
text: Works exactly as it should. I am using Amazon's new second layer verification system and the back up requires an app that scans a QR code to receive a random numeric password. This app works fine with my Fire Phone and Fire tablet. The permissions required are also considerably fewer than some of the other similar apps available.

title: Works easily on multiple devices, keeps me balanced and on budget
text: I love this app so much that I have used it on three different phones since 2012 and it's the first app I download when I move to a new phone.  This is a checkbook register that keeps your transactions sorted and categorized easily and the math balanced. It does NOT connect to or link to any bank account or have access to your actual bank which I like. I use this as a backup to my manual check book to double check my math and my automatic monthly transactions. Works perfectly on the Galaxy Note 1 and Note 3 and Fire Phone.

title: Easily the most concise and useful weather app available
text: Excellent weather app with no unnecessary extra nonsense. Clear radar both animated and still of whatever is headed your way. Plus the alerts for dangerous weather, a basic forecast and current conditions. That's all you need - no Twitter or Facebook book feeds to draw data or battery power. This is the best and easily most dependable weather app yet. It works on my Fire Phone, the Fire HDX Tablet and the Galaxy Note phones perfectly.

title: Excellent source for in-depth news around. the globe, works on Fire HDX tablet perfectly
text: This app works perfectly on both my Fire HDX tablets and my Fire Phone with no problems and the videos play correctly as well. Al Jazeera is unparalleled as a source for in-depth news beyond the American continent and the European basics. They always have a point of view on major U.S. stories that brings something new and different to the coverage from the usual sources.

title: Syncs perfectly with phone & tablets, voice training in app, remote functionality for the Echo
text:
This review is for the Echo APP that is the essential companion component to the actual physical Echo unit. The app is integral for full functionality of your Echo. You set up the WiFi connection, Bluetooth connections, create your iHeartRadio stations, access your shopping and to-do lists remotely and much more through the app. There is a very necessary voice training function through the app that lets you teach Alexa your particular speech patterns, inflections and cadence - this made a huge positive difference in the first week of using the Echo due to my deep Southern accent.

I have it loaded on my Fire Phone and two Fire Tablets and it syncs perfectly with anything going on with Alexa from anywhere. I received my Echo in November 2014 and have used it and the app daily ever since. A request of Alexa syncs through the app and shows up on my tablet, so I know if someone else in the house has added something to the shopping list immediately. It also appears on my list inside the app - this is very handy if I'm already at the grocery store. I haven't taken a written list to a store in months, I just open the app, tap each item on the list and go.

My favorite use for the app is remote practical jokery though. You can select any song from your library, Amazon Prime Music or a playlist and play it from anywhere. I was in another city while my Mother house sat for us and scared the pants off her having Alexa randomly play the JAWS theme and the Time Warp from the Rocky Horror Picture Show. As Amazon adds new functions to the Echo, the app upgrades regularly as well.

title: Works like a charm with Fire HDX 8.9 & Canon and Epson printers
text: Works perfectly with our Fire HDX 8.9 tablets. I installed this in Nov 2014 on two Fire tablets and use it for two different printers in our network and Google Drive. I had one small issue back in Nov and the customer service folks from the app responded to my email within just a few hours and solved my problem. The app has worked like a charm since then and I use it to print emails, documents, screen shots, photos etc every couple of days. Totally worth the money.

title: Perfect for Fire Phone and Fire HDX Tablets, Updates all day
text: I used this app and loved on my old Galaxy Note and it's working exactly the same on my Fire Phone and my Fire HDX 8.9 tablet.  News in all genres and categories from around the world and every source you could want in one simple place. And it updates all day long.

text: This is one of those silly things that just makes me happy. The two thumb controls on the screen work well once you get the hang of them and the response time is fast.  The graphics are good and the game is exactly as I remember from back in the day with the big bulky console and cartridge things. So glad to find this timewaster in a portable version for my Fire HDX!!

title: Works well on Fire HDX tablets & fully integrated with Amazon Echo
text: This is a pretty impressive free service. We discovered iHeartRadio through our Amazon Echo and now have the app on both of our Fire HDX tablets. The ease of creating a custom station, skipping songs and finding new music is great. The integration with Echo means I no longer have any need for Pandora whatsoever.

title: Easy to use, works perfectly on Fire HDX tablet
text: Works great on my Fire HDX tablet. Does all the basic math stuff and is very handy for percentages and slightly more complex math problems. Still excellent after a full year.

title: Works with Fire HDX tablet and WiFi Epson printer
text: Works with my Fire HDX 7 tablet perfectly. I am printing documents and photos from both my tablet and my Amazon cloud files. You do need this app and a compatible Epson WiFi printer.

title: Inaccurate for Texas and Louisiana
text: While this app does work with both my Fire HDX tablet and my Fire Phone, it just isn't as complete or accurate as the imbedded Amazon map program and it darn sure can't compete with Google's maps. Mapquest had me driving 37 miles out of my way compared to Amazon's map directions on my Fire Phone and 42 extra miles compared to Google maps on my laptop. Perhaps on the East or West Coasts it might be more accurate, but for the country roads and small towns of Texas, Louisiana, Arkansas and Mississippi, this just didn't work well at all. Deleted it.

I downloaded it specifically to help me get a better feel for using the dynamic perspective and getting comfortable with it. To-Fu Fury does that and is a nice problem solving/maze type game too. title: Works on LG V10 & Blu phones - convert any song to the perfect ring tone
text:
************ UPDATE - JUNE 30 2016 *******

I've moved from the Fire Phone to a new LG V10 and back into the traditional Android operation system and I'm still using this great app with my new phone. It works like a charm just as it always did with the Fire Phone and my husband is using it on his Blu Energy 2 phone now as well.
***************************************************

You can do multiple songs and set different ones for different contacts.

title: Works fine with MG5420 printer & Fire HDX

title: Easy to use, loads of good information for most of Cambodia
text: I used this app multiple times on our two week trip through Cambodia in May 2013. It has basic tourist tips and info for almost all the cities in Cambodia. One thing i really like about the app is the ability to update specific information on hotels & restaurants including photos on the go - helpful for the next person using the app behind you. I will look for other travel apps from this company for our next trip!

title: Does exactly what I need it to do ...
text:
A lifesaver! (well, probably not really, but still ... ) I've been so careful with my Fire Stick remote, but somehow the cat knocked a bunch of stuff off the end table and it disappeared (probably under the sofa or loveseat, but I need help to move the furniture).

I had downloaded the Fire TV Remote App when I first got my Fire Stick around 3 yrs ago or so, but forgot about it until I came online to order a replacement remote and noticed the note about the app. Fired it up on my iPhone, and it worked!!

It took a minute or two to remember who to navigate it, but I figured it out enough to get to Hulu, Netflix, etc. No problems at all!

Seriously, I don't think I need to order a new remote -- I should be able to do what I need to do with the app. Time will tell, but after 3 days using just the app to control the Fire Stick, I haven't found anything so far that needs the actual remote to accomplish. Very happy!

title: Finicky, works OK if you  s-p-e-a-k  s--l--o--w--l--y ...
text:
I had really high hopes for this. I guess I still do, sort of. But I've been working with it for nearly a month, and it's still very temperamental. This was my first experience with Dragon software, so I have no prior experience with their products.

First, I'm using it with a 2015 MacBook Air 13", OS X Yosemite 10.10.5, using Pages (I don't use MS Word). I've "played" with Apple's Dictation feature with decent results, but it's pretty basic. I'm originally from the midwest, and I've lived on the West Coast for over 35 years, so my accent is fairly "flat." Also, I'm using the Mac's built-in microphone -- no external "higher quality" mic for now, as I wanted to see how it would operate using standard equipment.

Installation went about as I expected (I worked in IT before I retired), so no major issues. I worked through the tutorial, again with no major issues. Dragon seemed to be able to understand what I was saying most of the time. It's relatively fast, too -- but I do find I have to slow down my normal speech pattern in order for it to understand. If I go slowly enough, I can get about 95% accuracy; if I speak "normally," it drops to 80-85%.

I guess the biggest problem I'm having is its tendency to misinterpret words for commands, or not understand commands that it's supposed to be able to handle. It works only sporadically -- even when I'm careful to enunciate clearly. This is where I hope to see some improvement over time. I may end up investing in a better microphone, too.

At this point, I'll keep using it for writing notes, reports, etc. It does seem to work reasonably well for that. But overall not enough to warrant more than 3 stars -- "It's OK" for now.

title: Had fun with this ...
text:
I'm a total newb when it comes to animation software -- or I WAS a total newb, until I got Anime Studio Debut. OK, so I'm STILL basically a newb, but I've been able to create a few animated shorts to go with some of the music I've written using Finale software on my MacBook Pro.

I didn't have too much trouble getting started with this, but did run into some of its restrictions as time progressed (e.g., you're restricted to creating no more than 2 minutes of animation). Still, for the money, and for an introduction to the Anime Studio products, this is a good deal. If you're looking for more than just a basic set of tools, you might want to opt for the Anime Studio Pro version ([[ASIN:B0052VKP0W Anime Studio Pro 8]]).

title: Echo Dot for the win!
text:
I love this thing. I contemplated between the Echo and Echo Dot for quite a while before I decided in the Dot. My house is set up with a stereo system that isn't blue tooth enabled, so the aux port on the Dot was the seller for me. At first I used the Dot for every little thing I could think of (you know, new toy kind of thing). But then I found myself continuing to use it. I set timers and alarms all the time, listen to music, catch the weather, play my audible books, and answering questions when I am in the middle of something and don't want to grab my phone (getting ready, working out, making dinner).

I am really looking forward to enabling some smart features on my house. I fall asleep with the light on when I am reading all the time. It would be nice, when I am so comfy, to just tell Alexa to turn off the lights. Or if it's hot in the house in the middle of the night, it would be cool to just tell Alexa to turn down the temp.

title: must have app for lovers of reading
text: This app was my gateway reading drug before getting a Kindle. Sometimes I'd go to bed and want to read, but not want to disturb with a light while my husband was sleeping. I would usually just play games on my phone, but sometimes it is simply reading that really hits the spot. So I downloaded this app and found some free books. I'd snuggle down into my pillows, blanket pulled up over my head, and cozily read in any position I wanted. I got so used to reading on my phone at night, that I ended up getting a Kindle. Huge deal since I had always been one of those snobs who eschewed digital reading in favor of a paper book. Now I have an added option that also adds great value to my life. There are books I would have never considered getting in physical copy, and certainly there are authors I would have never had the chance to read if it were not for e:books, namely all things on Kindle. This app (got it in 2011) was the catalyst for a whole new world of reading. I still have it on my phone as the app and device sync, so if I dont have my Kindle, I can just read from my phone. Excellent for waiting rooms for doctor appointments.

title: Seems like the perfect starting point for newbies to learn animation...
text: If you don't have access to a dvd rom, you can download from their site and just input the serial number from the dvd. I have zero knowledge about animations, but in navigating inside the software, it feels very familiar to photoshop. On the left side of the software is a BEGINNERS MODE TOOL column. When you highlight a tool, there is an info box explaining what the tool does, as well as a video link you can click on for further demonstration. These video links take you to YT btw. Get used to the word BONE. In animation, you need to give your character bones in order for them to move. Each bone, whether it be arm, leg, hand, etc. has movements that you can define. I still know nothing, but I feel the software isn't all that intimidating if you spend a little time with it, as well as watching YT tutorials. And if you have photoshop or adobe illustrator experience, I think you'll feel right at home. 5-Stars.

title: Video and photo editing software chock-full of features...

title: Who Needs that Other Illustration Program? Not Me!
text:
It took me a while to get started on this, Corel's answer to other professional illustration programs.It has many components and features. The performance varies slightly, depending on your computer's capabilities. As you probably suspect, the main difference is whether you are using Windows 10 with an active screen or another version with a mouse. I installed it on both a Windows 7 machine (with a marble mouse) and a Windows 10 laptop with an active screen. (I contacted customer service through the chat function on the Corel site and verified that I could install the software on up to 3 devices, as long as I did not use more than one at a time. You do, by the way, have to have an Internet connection to verify the installation, and you need to be online at least once a month to continue using it, so it does "check in". Customer service was prompt, polite, and helpful, so if you need it, it's there.) All of the components worked well on both machines.

I downloaded the bonus RAW processor AfterShot 3 too. I have Photoshop Elements and Canon's Digital Photo Professional 4 (free from the Canon site with my DSLR). They are all similar. Elements downloads from my camera. The other two access files already downloaded to files on my computer (in Libraries) by Windows. AfterShot3 adds a few functions to RAW editing. The one I have missed in the other two is healing. AfterShot does it, in a limited way, on a layer. In any case, AfterShot3, as included, isn't the current top of the line version of their software, but it is enough to alllow you to open RAW files, then to convert them to TIFFs or JPEGs for use with the illustration and web design components of DRAW 2017. As you can imagine, this suite of programs will take a while to master, unless you have used them before. (If you are used to Illustrator, it will be easier. Although things work a little differently, an Illustrator-like work space is provided.If Illustrator terrifies you, a basic work space especially designed for beginners is also available.) As usual with Corel products, the online help and documentation is great. There are both printed and video directions and hints. Downloading program components took a while on my DSL connection, but I'm used to it. That's the fault of our internet service here in rural Colorado, not Corel. So far, I have processed and converted a huge batch of RAW vacation captures from my Canon DSLR and created a cool chicken profile vector using my marble mouse on my Windows 7 machine. It's a lot easier than Illustrator (at least the version I have). It doesn't go nuts if you pause for a minute. I saved it as an AI file (many file formats are available). I plan to use the included font manager to help me fill it with witty text for a shirt. If you need a graphics program, but don't want to deal with a "cloud" subscription, check out the features of this new suite. Chances are, it has everything you need in an easy-to-learn, well supported package.

title: Old version works better
text: My husband uses an earlier version of this program along with the capture device from the same company, but every so often its bugs catch up with it. He has to uninstall and reinstall it. Okay, well, I have a new Windows 10 laptop, so I thought I'd give the program another chance. (I don't use capture, but I might use some other features of the program and I wanted to see if it worked better on the Windows 10 device. Unfortunately, I guess you only get one installation because it wouldn't accept the serial number. Don't bother with this. Look for an older version.

title: Consolidation at Last!
text: I have used McAfee virus protection for many years and it has always worked well for me. One problem has always been the multiple accounts that spring up when we purchase new laptops or desktops. Customer service straightens them out when you ask, but it's a bother. This single yearly subscription is a great solution. The box contains a wallet-sized card with a serial number on it and a larger card with directions in English on one side and French on the other directing you to an activation site. When you go to the site and enter the serial number, you are directed to input your account information or to create an account. It's very easy.The next step takes your computer a while (depending on the speed of your connection), but you can work while the program files are downloading. (It took about an hour on my slow DSL connection.) The installation on my Windows 7 computer was seamless. To install the program on other devices, including MACs, additional PCs, laptops, and mobile devices, you just open a provided McAfee email on the device and download the software. What a great idea!

title: Conserve Online Real Estate and Save Time with File Compression
text:
This new version of a classic program does what it is supposed to do. It installed easily on my Windows 7 laptop. It zipped and unzipped sample files with ease. If you use cloud services (SkyDrive, Dropbox, Google Drive,CoudMe, and SugarSync), this will save you upload and download time, as well as storage fees. Links in the program make sharing easier too. Zipping large files can save space on your own hard drives and the program makes that easy. The program will resize images as well as text files, but I think you should use dedicated image programs, even free ones, for resizing.
This is the basic edition.The Pro edition offers some features I would love to have, such as PDF backups of email. This is not the Pro edition and it does not include these features. It will open self-extracting files, but it won't create them.If you distribute software, you will need more advanced version. Recommended for those who share a lot of files to Facebook, Twitter, and LinkedIn, as well as the Cloud.

title: A few minutes is all you need to set your mind at ease! I recommend it.
text:
This is a fantastic product! I put off using it for a while after it came. It's kind of a grim thing to think about, but we are all mortal, and it's very important to make sure this kind of business is taken care of. I thought it would be complicated and that I would need a lot of information to get started. Not so!
When I opened the box, I found not only the CD with the software (Windows XP/Vista/7/8,), but also the handy, easy-to-understand handbook from NoLo, The Legal Answer Book for Families with basic advice from lawyers relating to marriage, divorce, child custody, child support, adoption, children, and elder care.
This information will be very useful for people with large families and complex issues related to their estates. Our current situation is very simple, so I did not need it to set up a will, but I am glad to have it as a reference.
NoLo's self-help legal guides have worked for my husband and me before. We handled his mother's estate, which turned out to be more complex than we had expected, using one of their books, and everything went well.
To use the software, simply load it into your computer, register it, and then answer some very simple interview questions. Then, hit print, and all the documents you need for your state and county will print out, ready to be witnessed and signed. That's it. You can also print out record pages to collect and keep track of your assets. We are going to do that when we prepare our taxes in January.
As a bonus, you get a free one year subscription to NoLo's Living Trust service online.
We have been worried about this particular task for a long time. It seemed so complicated. It was a relief to see how easy it was. A little time now (a few minutes) can save many people a lot of money and grief later. I recommend it!

On older systems, I can see the difference once PC Tuneup has run. And it has stopped a lot of crap. I am often the only one NOT affected by a virus! There are two things that you must know, however:

1. There is NO LICENSE KEY on the device!! Many people have trouble because it installs a trial of premium and then asks you to pay again after a few days, or it installs the free version. title: Easy to use; could use a format update

It warns you repeatedly to always double check the price before purchasing, but I have only found the price NOT to be free once.  I like that the free books are updated daily, so there's always something new to try.  But the format is a little hard to look at--not sure why it needs to say "Free Tap to Download FREE" next to each title; that should be obvious to anyone that has downloaded this app on their Kindle.  I wish the books were listed by category so I wouldn't have to wade through the entire list every time I use the app.  It skips from horror to Christian to occult to children's to self-improvement.  I am glad the categories are at least listed with the titles though.  There are always repeats within the list as well, so sorting through the titles can be kinda annoying, but for a free app for free Kindle ebooks, I haven't found anything better.

title: Sick of Paying Subscription Fees?  Me too...
text:
While I understand the reason behind Microsoft’s going to a subscription model for Office, I’m simply sick of having so many different pieces of software that require a subscription in order for me to use them.

I had used WordPerfect many years ago and when I saw how this version of WordPerfect had support for 60 file types I decided it was time for me to give it a try again.

As someone who has worked exclusively within the Office Suite for many years there are some differences but they are minor and didn’t take long for me to figure out. Corel knows users are more familiar with the Office software so they have worked to make this as intuitive as possible without violating any copyright.

The one area where this software really lags behind Office is in spreadsheets. Depending on how often you use this type of thing that would be something to consider. Since I don’t use it too frequently, although I do a bit, I’ve made it work with effort.

In terms of the word processing program which is what I primarily use, it’s excellent and I have no complaints. Use is straightforward, easy to figure out, quick to find what you need, lots of built in templates and best of all - no ongoing subscription fee to pay!

I’ve been very satisfied with this product after using it for the last couple of months and have recommended it to several friends as I will to you as well.

title: Panda is great.

I had one minor issue this year so I Panda e-mailed tech support and they responded with the correct solution within the day.  I couldn't ask for more.  My advice is go for the previous year's version (last year's from when you are buying - not the newest Panda) and save the money as you will get the same updates (both for the anti-virus engine and the viruses) for much less $$$.  As a long time user the program does not change a great deal year to year other than cosmetically - so save the money.  In addition I will add that I would always spend the few extra dollars for the 3 PC option as it will allow you to install it on 2 additional computers other than your own - a very nice feature to have...

title: Easy-peasy to use
text:
One of the easiest to use photo editing programs I've ever used.

You do get a disc, but you can download it and use the key that comes with the disc. Once loaded, you can choose what will open in this program. I chose photos not realizing that it would also connect to Painter and try to open all my riff files. So when/if this happens, it is easily fixed.

If you have Painter, you have to go into the properties of one of your RIFF images, click on open with Painter because this program will try to open your riffs, and it steals all the riff previews with a little blue camera. Easily fixed in the properties.

If you don't have Painter, then no problems. You can do everything this program promises. Although, the auto fix isn't as clever as it promises. Sometimes it works very well, and other times it makes the photo way too bright and washes out the image. But then, you probably don't want auto fix if you bought this program, right?

I love how you can compare your fixes to the original side by side. Also, you can find tutorials online, and the instructions are so easy to read and helpful when you want to do a particular thing with your photos.

Another thing I really love is that you can add watermarks to your images. I do a lot of painting with Painter. I can add a watermark with PaintShop Pro in a few seconds. You can also add text using your own system fonts. Very versatile and very easy to use.

title: Had Problems Getting It Started
text: I thought this would be easy because these types of learning CD's have been around for years.  However, when I tried to load it to my personal laptop with Windows XP Home Edition V5.1 with SP2 and Windows Media Player 11, I had all kinds of issues.  First it took over an hour to activate while connected to the Internet (note brief instructions in the CD say it can take several minutes). I had to activate it several times, until I was prompted to install a component for the Windows Media Player.  Once I got this installed, I was able to restart and move around the training sections.  The training content and presenter is good, but the tool could be made easier to navigate through the sections.  There are some other Crystal Reports XI training cds on the Web for around $100.  I may actually purchase one of those as well to see if there is anything else I can learn about Crystal Reports.  One includes a .pdf of the training, which would be helpful.  I give this product a "4" because of the high price and the issues I had getting it up and running.  However, it's a much better alternative than sitting in a classroom and paying about four to five times as much money to learn.  Plus, when you are actually working on reports, it's nice to have a tool like this to pull up the section and troubleshoot.

title: software becoming a challenge to use
text: for many DIY tax return customers, this software is becoming a challenge to use.

title: Turbo Tax software requires Windows 10 and hence BE AWARE.
text: Turbo Tax software requires Windows 10 and hence BE AWARE.  I am a big fan of Turbo Tax software, and urge everyone to try doing their own taxes.  Its an real education and skill.  Yet, you should be aware that software is very "temperamental."  Really.  Each step,  you must complete the questions.  No excuses.

title: good luck if your computer is older
text:
Requires Window 7. So, good luck if your computer is older.

Turbo tax is an extension of health care enforcement as it asked "Do you have health care?" That was 2015 and 2016. A bit personal, right?

Anyway, the ability to do your own taxes is awesome. Good to get this early in your lifetime. Pass along your data from year to year. Keep PDF copy of filings. Save a ton of paper.

Very good if you have accounting skills.

title: Federal IRS taxes, State IRS taxes, an AUDIT included
text:
Intuit turbo tax has been selling this software for last 20 years. And, I have been using it for just as long. The price can be a little cheaper, now that millions are using it, but whatever. The real value of this software comes in year two, year three, etc. Because the software IMPORTS your prior year information, and populates the data elements into this year, it really does get better year after year. You may want to chance and buy the DELUXE version. That is good enough for 99% people. The PREMIER version has too many bells and whistles and not enough user defined control elements.

The state filing is also easy, because that too gets transferred data from prior entries. You must select a STATE upon loading the software onto your laptop or desktop computer. Please, please, please ... follow the directions and questions before hitting the YES or NO button. You could cause an error if you press the wrong button. Remember, you can redo at any step. And this saves you so much time in recalculating numbers.

So, I am no longer a victim to errors, mistakes, poor data entry, and other stupid calculation errors because the software comes with ability to GO BACK and re-do steps, and then again at the very end, with a personal AUDIT check. That is your personal audit check (do not get scared, because it is just) on your laptop computer where the program is running, telling you -- hey, you made an error. Once you fix the errors, you can resubmit for another audit check. No extra charge. When everything passes, your tax returns are in compliance to be submitted.

If you prefer to bring your taxes to an accountant, you will probably still want to check against this software. I have been using this software for many years. And, on occasions provided intuit with my feedback. One of which was to allow users to REPRINT the form from a PDF output file. That feedback was back in 1996 or so. And, they did. Turbo tax has a PDF file output of your tax return pages, with forms and everything. You just need to sign it. Cool. title: Do not try to migrate from OLDER VERSION to this one.
text:
Ghost 12 software cannot read older versions of Ghost "backup data" so do not remove your older versions, and do not try to migrate over to Ghost 12. You run the risk of having to restore your older software just so you can recover from your older backup. Too much trouble - so if you have an old Ghost 2003, just keep it on your old machine. Furthermore, just use the Ghost 12 "restores" only on your new machine, running "backups" created by Ghosts 12 from your Vista operating system.

Keep them seperate, for now, until Symantec fixes the bugs.

Bad News
--------
I have opened a trouble ticket with NORTON SYMANTEC CO. about this issue. As for me, I was very disappointed about the bug. But, I continue to have faith in their products. Of course, some of the reviews has made me a bit cautious. (My god, can the reviews be bad.) Ok, I agree, bugs like these should have been fixed BEFORE release. It should not have to be field tested and pain inflicted onto customers. So, you are right to be upset (those above who gave SYMANTEC less than 4-star).

I am taking one star away, because they should have al least WARNED me about backward incompatible issues BEFORE sale of the software.

Good News
---------
I have ghost 2003 on my old machine and it has been a life saver. I have often found "free" downloaded third party software comes with bugs. And as often, I have had to reach down into Norton Ghosts - and restore my operating system. Wonderful to have such a software feature. Thanks to Norton Ghosts. The 2003 version was a really nice product. So, I give the system the 4 star rating - because I hope that the bugs in ghost 12 will eventually be resolved.

Specially the DISASTER RECOVERY module, which allows me to boot from disk and restore the entire operating system - when the system crashes. Oh my God, what a life saver that was. I can tell you countless stories when Peter Norton has saved my life, in the past. Even with his UNDELETE programs from good old DOS days. So, lets give him a break, ok?

As a matter of fact, what ever happened to the Undelete? Anyway ...

I have Ghost 12 on my new Vista OS machine, and for the few times in use - the backup seemed to work fine. My expectation is that Peter Norton must WAKE-UP and fix these bugs, and eventually in the LIVE UPDATE, a patch will appear and all my machines will be operating in very happy mode.

If you are reading this far ... be happy and the world will follow you.

Nobody from prior reviews suggested using Microsoft backup. Ha, ha, ha.

title: Some serious missing connections
text: Let me start by saying that I am a loyal TurboTax customer for the past 14 years.  That said, there are those rare occasions when I have had a financial transaction or a set of circumstances that is beyond what the average taxpayer experiences in their typical financial lives. It is when these situations have occurred that I have run into trouble with TurboTax.  This past year it was an Employee Stock Purchase share sales transaction.  The Premier product marketing factoid actually promotes that the Premier version has the capability to walk a taxpayer through this type of transaction.  Wrong answer!  As with past footnote type of experiences,  the software has the capability to capture this unique event however, it took a tax professional (one of the thousands of very helpful and knowledgable CPA's that Intuit employs) to help me understand how to capture the ESPP stock sale transaction.  So the three stars are for the software itself.  As for the tax professionals that helped me out of these messes I have to give them five stars all the way.  So the only drawback is if 4 hours of hold time and being screened by a less knowledgable tax "professional" before getting the answer to my question is a problem for the potential consumers of this product.  For me, I have been more than satisfied with the overall result. I have only had to consult the professionals three times in 14 years.  I believe in product reviews and I know i have appreciated some of the more in depth reviews that I have come across.  I hope this review is helpful to someone.

title: Netflix watchability going downhill fast.
text:
The app works fine, although I rarely use it. I usually watch Netflix through Roku. However, Netflix itself is my main gripe here. It recently removed the ability to instantly see user reviews on its programs. Which means I have to do 2-3 extra clicks in order to avoid wasting time on watching some horrible one star show or movie. So, instead of being able to browse and watch movies freely, we now have to play the "Where Are The Reviews" game in order to get where we want to go. I don't care how much percent a given program matches their perception of my tastes. Their algorithm sucks. It's way off from my tastes. I would much rather have an inkling (quickly) of what my peers think of a given show/movie/program.

It was already bad enough when they added the uncalled-for (non-optional) jarring program reviews, which happen whenever you rest on any program square for more than a few seconds. The mini-reviews are noisy, obtrusive and jarring. Not at all needed or welcome.

The whole thing is getting to be more trouble than it's worth. It was already hard enough to find some decent content worth watching. Now here we are spending more time than ever searching, rather than enjoying. And the searching is not pleasant to do. Amazon can be hard to navigate, but we get lots of really good content with Amazon Prime programming. I have been watching more of that and am thinking seriously of just discontinuing Netflix altogether.

title: This is not your mother's WordPerfect!  Excellent Software Program!
text:
This is definitely not your mother's WordPerfect. WordPerfect is one of the first word documents software available. However, you will notice that this is similar to Microsoft Word. Corel has made many software products, and they have a sustained reputation with software users.

I have installed this on Windows 8.1. There was an easy setup with two discs. I started using a table and although creating the table was easy, I was not able to click on a www link and have it automatically take me to the site. But, I managed to work with it anyway.

I love the step by step tutorials in the HELP REFERENCE CENTER. This is vital with any software program. And for further information you can log into Wordperfect.com and Corel. You can log into more tutorials if needed.

Publishing to PDF is an important feature. Anymore, these days, we are always sending files and preserving the file in PDF is critical for the receiver or for another device. This program has a usable friendly feature for using and creating PDFs. The capabilities are limited, but user friendly for those who do not need excessive PDF features.

Now, mail merge can be challenging, if you have old Word programs, however, with this WordPerfect, the steps are very easy. Macros... another improvement from Older Word programs.

Also, other great features with WordPerfect are the e-book publishing software....so many people can certainly use this, as this is the era of self-publishing. The disc burning software is a wonderful feature.

As I mentioned, if you have used only outdated WordPerfect programs, you will be quite impressed with the Word Perfect Office x7 Software Program.... This is amazing with so many capabilities.. Rizzo

title: Not much step by step training, help is difficult to locate, great editing, viewing and voice reading
text:
I do have some experience with PDFs, but this program can be complex for the beginner. Although this program has some great features for Converting and Editing your PDFs, learning will be challenging. What I discover is that there is really not a good training for the program. Help is too difficult to work with.

The Reference Guide is 42 pages in PDF format, so it is easier to manipulate the pages, meaning you can also run pages side by side, continuous pages, zoom in, etc. etc. However, don't expect the ins and outs of buttons, definitions, how to, etc. I do not find it user friendly with info like: PDF Creation which has info like Location, Source Files, Conversion Method and Target. This tells you the steps to Create PDFs from different sources, like Word, Excel, Powerpoint, handling mixed files, and how do PDF Files work? Not some learning info I need.
You will find a bit of info on the Toolbar.

So, where do I got for some step by step help? Click on the HELP tab and go searching using the Contents, Index, and Search. Not the best, but anything will do. Basically, any great training steps are difficult to locate.

What I really like is the number of tools available for editing PDFs, such as the Cropping, highlighting, adding notes, comments, graphics, strikeouts, callouts, text box and creating signatures, which I am having trouble finding the easy steps to this.

Reading a PDF book can be enlightning; turning on the voice, a toolbar for the comments, ability to notetake, etc. What is also a very nice feature which is included in the graphics is adding Watermarks, which can read, Draft, Confidential, Reviewed, etc., etc.,

Now, there is a 1 GB flash drive with examples but this too lacks basic training or description.

Again, this is not an easy program to learn. The training lacks plenty, as it is not easy to locate good step by step instructions. Once you learn it, you can do so much!! Rizzo

title: And I thought WordPerfect was dead long ago!
text:
To my surprise, I have discovered that WordPerfect is NOT dead! It can only get better from that antiquated software of the early 90s, and Microsoft blew them away. But, I guess there is reincarnation!

I like the program, and what I like is that one can open this to be strictly WordPerfect style OR, get this...you can have it open this in a Microsoft Word mode. Excellent option! And when I become more acquainted, I may choose the WordPerfect mode.

But I can't help but think that these software programs should create simple, easier to use, usable programs. The average user will never get to some sections. And why would we need the And you need to purchase the impressive Corel Visual Intelligence, which is mainly geared for businesses.

The WordPerfect Lighting for creating notes seems redundant to a Word doc, and I see this program as unnecessary, not extremely beneficial. For a busy Word professional, I'm quite sure it can be helpful, or if you have nothing better to do, go for it.

There are many neat features in WordPerfect, so many that one needs to experiment with and compare to Microsoft Word. Excel too, has some great benefits and features that you have not seen in Word, and with time, you will learn them.

If you want to incorporate your email setup to the WordPerfect email, you have that option.

You can learn a lot from this WordPerfect program, unfortunately, the average user doesn't have a whole lot of time for learning more software, new software, or complicated software. But all in all, this is NICE !!.....Rizzo
