title: Review of Sage Timeslips 2019, Discs Version, 2 users
text:
Sage Timeslips software has been around for more than 25 years. The Sage corporation itself has been in business for over 38 years. It specializes in accounting software for small and medium businesses. The types of industries their software is most helpful for include: professionals (especially lawyers), construction, manufacturing, wholesale distribution, chemicals, food and beverage, and non-profits. Sage offers not only desktop software, but cloud accounting as well. On their corporate website, they offer self-serve customer support for this software in the following forms:

—Sage University offers online, on-demand instructor-led classes.
—Sage Knowledgebase offers 24/7 access to product documentation, informative articles, and the latest software downloads and updates.
—Sage City is a place to connect with sage pros, industry peers and certified partners of Sage in order to ask questions and learn useful tips and tricks to optimize one’s benefits from the software.
—Sage Resources offers additional ways to get your questions answered.

Note that this particular software only works with Windows. It is not something that one can sit down and start using within five minutes, unless one is an IT professional, such as my husband, who is gifted at intuitively grasping the basic workings of software, any software, within seconds. I would imagine that averagely tech-literate users might learn this software’s basic functions within a day or two. But non-techie types might be struggling for weeks, or even months, to learn it—and only it they have access to outside assistance for training.

Similar to software such as Excel and Word for Windows, this software can be used in a very basic way, but there are many advanced features that more ambitious users might find valuable to master. One way to do that, beyond the free support from Sage’s website which I mention above, is the book, “Sage Timeslips for Dummies.” Or, if you can afford it, direct customer support is sold separately from this software itself. If you are not very tech savvy, it might be a good idea to purchase the tech-support package offered by Sage in order to get access to setup assistance and full product support for at least a few months after purchasing this software. I got this software free through Vine, and I cannot afford to purchase Sage’s tech-support package. Therefore, I can’t personally comment on whether their tech support is competent and/or a good value for the money.

With Timeslips, it is not necessary to have a “one size fits all” approach to handling the billing of various clients. Different clients can have a range of hourly rates embedded in specially tailored formats for their final bill. It is possible to create pre-bills in order to edit them before sending out final bills. The program also helps guide users through important aspects of a given project that would ideally be tracked. It helps users stay organized, including calendaring deadlines for phases of a given project.

This particular software is known as the “Timeslips Perpetual.” It is a traditional, licensed piece of software which the buyer acquires through a one-time purchase, and it is owned “in perpetuity.” This means the buyer is licensed to use this software, with no further license fees, for as long as it will operate on the currently available Windows operating systems. As stated above, no support services are included if you purchase this particular software, but you can purchase optional support contracts directly from Sage.

In contrast, Timeslips Premium is a subscription-based product. During the term of the software subscription, the user is eligible to receive all new releases of the product, both updates and new editions. It includes access to Sage Technical Support at no extra charge, and it provides some extra features that are not included in this particular Perpetual version of the Timeslips software. You have to renew your subscription annually, and if you cancel, you will lose access to your database within 30 days of non-renewal. That loss of data, to my mind, is a major drawback of the Premium version.

The Timeslips Premium version might be the better choice for companies to which these situations apply:

—The company has more than 15 potential users and expects to add more as time goes on.
—The company will need training services and technical support on an ongoing basis to train and assist its staff who are using the software.
—Management in the company puts an emphasis on staying on top of the latest and greatest in accounting software.
—The company wishes to avoid the very steep, upfront costs of outright purchase of this software. The Premium version’s upfront costs are about 20-25% of the cost of purchasing a Perpetual license. But, of course, over time, the subscription fees of the Premium version will end up costing a whole lot more than the cost of outright purchase of the Perpetual version.

The Timeslips Perpetual version might be a better choice for self-employed individuals or very small companies to which these situations apply:

—The individual or company has no problem with sticking with existing software for up to 4-5 years, without an update.
—The individual or company does not need training or support, or is willing to pay extra for it a la carte.
—The individual or company has no plans to expand their staff and will need no extra user licenses.
—The individual or company can’t afford the high cost of outright purchase of the Perpetual version.

There is a downside to purchasing a Timeslips Perpetual version such as this product:

—After only 18 months, Sage will no longer sell a la carte technical support for this product. After that, the owner/user would have to rely on the other sources of product information listed above.
—After 18 months, Sage will no longer sell additional user licenses for the version of this product one has purchased. By that time, Sage will have labeled your Perpetual version, “obsolete.”
—After 18 months, Sage will no longer support integration of the Timeslips Perpetual version with add-on products like their Timeslips eCenter.

It is possible to convert one’s Perpetual license to a Premium subscription. It merely involves contacting Sage, giving them more money, and they will change the license key embedded in your Perpetual version. But, as mentioned above, it wouldn’t be cost effective to do this until at least 4-5 years have passed after laying down the whopping cost to purchase a Perpetual license.

It is also possible, at least currently, to convert one’s Premium subscription to a Perpetual license. It involves purchasing, at full price (such as the price for this particular software), a full license to Timeslips, and confirming with Sage a relevant license key change. This route provides a smaller risk than instantly purchasing the Perpetual version. You try out this software first as a Premium subscription, with all the bundled-in training and updates that gives you, and then, if you have learned all you need to, you switch to Perpetual and are all set for 4-5 years.

One final note of warning: There is a movement in the software industry, in general, toward solely offering subscription-based licenses. Thus, there may come a time within a few years that a Perpetual license, such as this particular product offers, may no longer be available from Sage.

I rate this product as follows:

User friendly AKA ease of use: 3 stars
Flexibility of business applications: 5 stars
Customer support: 3 stars
Value for Money: 4 stars
Overall: 3.8 rounded to 4 stars

title: This accounting software is one among multiple, viable choices
text:
Sage accounting software has three options, ranging from least to most expensive: Pro, Premium and Quantum. This is the Premium plan. You pay a fee to purchase it, rather than a subscription, and many other accounting software packages only offer subscriptions these days. Note that tech support is not free.

Positives:
--Most people will find this software fairly easy to install.
--It tracks billing and produces invoices.
--It tracks inventory.
--It can integrate with your online banking.
--It tracks and manages costs of various jobs.
--It tracks overall cash flow
--It integrates with Microsoft Word to link up correspondence and reports to financial records.

Negatives:
--It is virtually impossible to get a person on the phone when you call tech support, and their promised callbacks do not materialize.
--It cannot do payroll.
--Upgrades are priced much too high, and it's a particularly bad deal for the customer to be charged for lousy tech support.

Before you purchase any accounting software for your business, try asking your accounting what they advise. Below are some potential alternatives:

QuickBooks Online is the most popular accounting software for small businesses, and it can scale upwards as your business grows.

FreshBooks is useful to lone entrepreneurs and freelancers, in particular people who must track their billable hours and send an invoice to their customers.

Xero is often selected by business owners who desire a viable, less expensive alternative to QuickBooks Online and have fewer than five employees.

Wave is a decently functional accounting software which is chosen by many businesses operating on a shoestring budged because it is FREE.

title: This single-user accounting software is one among multiple, viable choices
text:
Sage accounting software has three options, ranging from least to most expensive: Pro, Premium and Quantum. This is the Pro plan, and it is licensed for only one user. The Premium and Quantum plans have fancier features, and they vary in price for that reason as well as the number of users who are licensed by a given business to access the software.

Sage 50 Pro Accounting 2019 contains the following features:

--Accounts payable (invoices and checks).
--Accounts receivable (tracking sales, inventory and customer payments).
--Payroll.
--Customizable reports.
--Budgeting and cash flow.
--Organizing phases of jobs in systematic steps, including expenses and revenues.

On the plus side, if you have a basic background in accounting or bookkeeping, you will probably find it easy to learn this software. All versions of the Sage accounting software are updated every year, but it is not as essential to purchase the new edition every year as it is for tax software.

Once you know how to use it, you will be unlikely to need further technical assistance. Also, if you purchase the current year's version toward the end of the year, a month or two before the next year's version comes out, you may be able to buy it through some venues at a substantially reduced price.

On the negative side, if you do NOT have a basic background in accounting or bookkeeping, you may strugle to learn this software. If you do not upgrade every year, you will not continue to receive technical support from Sage, and upgrading can be quite expensive for a small business.

Since this version of the software is for a single user, however, and presumably you would be planning to start using it as soon as you buy it, I can't see that this would be a major drawback.

Overall, this is a decent, basic accounting software, but rather expensive for what it does. You might be able to find a similar type of software for less money. Or, if money is not the issue, you might want another brand which is more popular because it is actually better. Below are some other potential alternatives:

QuickBooks Online is the most popular accounting software for small businesses, and it can scale upwards as your business grows.

FreshBooks is useful to lone entrepreneurs and freelancers, in particular people who must track their billable hours and send an invoice to their customers.

Xero is often selected by business owners who desire a viable, less expensive alternative to QuickBooks Online and have fewer than five employees.

Wave is a decently functional accounting software which is chosen by many businesses operating on a shoestring budged because it is FREE.

title: Excellent, legal, speech-recognition software
text:
Dragon speech recognition software has been around for 20 years, and in that time there have been many updates and improvements, including the addition of specialized software such as medical and legal. This particular version of Dragon Legal has been formulated specifically to recognize legal jargon. This 15.0 version has improved significantly in speech recognition accuracy and speed compared to the 12.0 version. It takes a bit of time to train the software to recognize your particular voice, but it's worth it. In addition, it's not very much more difficult to also learn and use Dragon's efficient and time-saving commands and macros.

The seller claims that it offers tech support for installation, setup, and product training. Given that my husband is a computer programmer, I haven't needed to call on their help, so I cannot personally vouch for it.

This software requires the following specifications in order to run adequately:

RAM: 4GB is listed as viable for running this software, but in reality, the bare minimum you need is 8GB. If you want optimal functionality, you should really have 16GB minimum, and for ideal, fast speed of processing, you should have 32GB RAM.
CPU: Intel® dual core or equivalent AMD processor. Faster processors yield faster performance. The seller recommends Intel i9, i7 or i5 processors.
Hard Disk Space: This program requires at least 4GB hard disk space.
Supported Operating Systems: Windows 7, 8.1, 10 (32- and 64-bit); Windows Server 2008 R2 & 2012 R2.
Online Tech Support: You need Internet Explorer 11 or higher or the current version of Chrome (other than the Nuance website, there are also many Dragon user forums online that are quite helpful).
Sound Card: You need a sound card that supports 16-bit recording.
Microphone: You need a built-in microphone or a Nuance-approved microphone. You can find out what those are by going to the Nuance website.
Internet: You need an Internet connection for product download and automatic product activation.

title: It works well after an initial extensive investment of your time
text:
Nuance's Dragon voice recognition software has been around for many years and is the gold standard for PC use. You can dictate just about anything. It is adaptable to individual speech patterns with up to 99% accuracy, but only after you spend time and effort training it, and you also have to master the software's many commands. In other words, Dragon will definitely, ultimately save you time, but only after you first invest quite a bit of your time learning the software and training it to recognize your unique voice patterns.

On the other hand, if you are fortunate enough to have very simple, non-business-related transcription needs, such as merely sending texts and writing emails, I would suggest instead of this software using the free, voice-recognition capability on your smartphone. It is infinitely more flexible, requires no extensive training, and is quite portable compared to Dragon.

title: Seems to work well
text:
I have owned for some time a previous version of Dragon Naturally speaking, Premium 12, which I purchased in 2013 for $235. (It currently sells for $74.) I found it onerous to train and use and not nearly as sophisticated as the simple, FREE voice recognition app on my smartphone which I have used for the past two years. That app works with about 80% accuracy with zero training. However, it has only a couple of simple editing commands and it is only good for the phone and not for other software applications.

This Dragon Professional Individual 15 promises to provide up to 99% accuracy and offers a series of simple voice commands for punctuation purposes. You can also customize your routine usage of specific technical jargon and certain proper names. It supports a specific list of voice recorders and smartphones for greater flexibility.

It's a lot faster dictating than typing (unless you are a really fast typist, of course). You can also make edits and utilize special formatting using your voice, for example, underlining, capitals and bolding.

A program like this isn't just for people who can't type, people with disabilities that prevent them from typing, or people who are excruciatingly slow at typing. It is also useful for fast typists who are frequently in situations where typing is not possible.

This program is simple to install and run setup. The voice recognition capabilities seem more sophisticated than for the Premium 12 and there is less need to do corrections by hand. It works well with Windows 10.

Depending on how elaborate your dictation needs are, it may well be that Premium 12 is sufficient for your needs. It certainly costs a lot less.

title: Excellent program
text:
I've been using TurboTax every year for the past 15 years or so. It makes doing one's own taxes extremely simple. I always do the complete walk-through, allowing the program to ask me every possible question about what I might need to report as well as any tax write-offs I might be eligible for. Doing that gives me the confidence that nothing vital has been overlooked.

This program makes online filing very easy. I complete and file my taxes the very day I get all my tax forms, between the last week of January and the first week of February. By doing that, I get my tax refund deposited in my checking account within a couple of weeks. It also thwarts identity thieves who might attempt to file a fake federal tax return in my name, which is a particularly scary type of identity theft.

I will definitely continue using this program and will get version 2018 when it is available.

title: Everyone should have a will, and this makes it easy to create your own
text:
This software can save you hundreds of dollars in legal fees for a will, and thousands of dollars for a living trust. It also provides you with other important estate documents, including a Health Care Directive AKA Living Will (which every hospital will ask you if you have when you go in for surgery) and a Durable Power of Attorney for Finance (a crucial document if you are incapacitated and need someone you trust to handle your money).

Note that this software offers you a free one year subscription to Nolo's Online Living Trust, dating from the time you purchase this software. It is easy to procrastinate, so don't let this valuable adjunct capability go to waste.

Note this warning from Nolo: "Free legal updates will keep your program current through 2018. Documents reflect the laws of your state. Estate planning documents are not valid in Canada, Louisiana or U.S.Territories."

title: Excellent alternative to Adobe Photoshop for non-professionals
text:
I had no problems loading this program to my desktop via DVD. I understand there is also the possibility of receiving it as a download version via Amazon. I would happily recommend this basic editing software to anyone just getting started in the adventure of editing their photos and videos.

If you are like my husband, who is an IT professional, you will probably, via fiddling around with it, master the capabilities of this program in no time at all. As for non-techie me, I needed help figuring it out, and I went to YouTube to watch beginner instructional videos on this software. I had a bit of a learning curve but since that seems to be an typical issue for me (and most ordinary people) when I am learning any new software, I am not downgrading my rating for this program due to that.

A very big advantage of this software is that it is a far less expensive option for non-professionals than using Adobe Photoshop.

This company also offers free phone support if you have installation issues, and they would no doubt give you support for other problems as well. See comments from the company to some of the 1-star reviews for contact information.

title: A reasonably priced, user-friendly, video-editing software
text:
I had never tried to edit a video before testing this program, so it is fortunate that this is not an extremely complex one. In other words, it is not a professional-level video editor. However, since few home users need that degree of capability, this is fine for such purposes, and the price is reasonable. Note that you can see tutorials on YouTube if the instructions that come with this software are not sufficient for you.

The program is loaded in a standard way, from a CD. There is a user guide available on Disc 1, but you can also follow a link to the manufacturer's website and download a PDF of the guide, if you prefer an electronic version (I do). It's a good idea to follow this link no matter what, because it's there that you get updates to this software and bug fixes.

The program is fairly user friendly. You can use this program to edit precious family videos or to create videos to upload to your website or to YouTube or Facebook. This program has specific capability for the latter two. You can also capture stills from your videos.

All in all, this is a fun program to play with, and I have no reservations about recommending it.

title: Terrific, highly useful app
text:
This app is extremely effective and powerful, especially considering I got it free.

This app has the ability to record any activities you wish, by any category or subcategory you choose to designate, and you can also type in an explanatory memo for any individual entry.

When you create categories, the app allows you to color code each category and assign an icon, which is visually very helpful.

This app can generate reports for any categories you choose, for any range of dates you desire. These reports can be exported to yourself or others, whoever you designate, via email, in either HTML or CSV (Excel) format.

I've used this app on my iPhone 5c for almost three years. I am what one might call a "power user," as I have used it consistently, recording essentially the whole day's activities, every day of those years. Because of this I've accumulated a huge amount of data in the database file within the program. The more one uses a program like this, the more important the ability to backup one's data becomes.

With this app, you can backup your data via several means: (1) synchronize with the developer's website, (2) do a routine backup of the entire contents of your smart phone, or (3) create a backup within the program which you can then copy off the phone into particular folder on your computer that you designate yourself.

I have had reason, several times, to contact the developer with questions about this app, and he has responded quite rapidly, often within hours, and he has been very friendly and helpful.

All in all, this is a terrific app!

title: Cost effective way to create a will and one living trust
text:
This Nolo software is a really simple, cost effective way to create a will. Far too many people, especially people with underage children, fail to create a will because they think they can't afford the cost of an attorney. With this affordable software, you don't need an attorney. Wills do not have to be filed with a court, simply secured in a safe place in the event of your demise, so there are no court costs.

This software is especially helpful in that it allows you to tailor your will to the laws of your state of residence, but ideally one would choose to follow the strictest standards of any state in the USA because the vast majority of Americans will not reside in the same location all their lives. These are some of the issues that are crucial in the creation of a will:

The testator is the legal term for the person who creates a will. In most states the testator must be 18 years of age, must be of sound mind, and voluntarily creating and signing a written will. Most states require at least two witnesses to a will, and that the witnesses be "disinterested," that is, not named to inherit in the will. Many states also require the testator to create a self-proving will. A self-proving will contains notarized signatures of the witnesses. The benefit of such a will is that it allows the probate court to avoid having to track down witnesses to the will. This is especially crucial in that if the probate court ever needs to call the witnesses to court, they may be hard to locate because they may have moved, or in the case of strangers witnessing a will signing, they might not even remember signing the will as witnesses at the time they are called to court. When a will is self-proved, these issues are extremely unlikely to arise, and also there is virtually no risk that an attempt to break a will will suceed via the ruse of claiming that the signature of the testator is a forgery.

With this particular software, in addition to a simple will, you can also create a healthcare directive, durable power of attorney for finances and other essential documents such as revocation documents, a property worksheet, a document including iInformation for caregivers and survivors, a statement of preferentces for final arrangements, and a sample form to create a letter to survivors. This software also provides executor documents, including an affidavit of domicile, a general notice of death, and an executor's checklist. There are personal finance documents such as a general bill of sale, security agreement for borrowing money, promissory notes, and a limited power of attorney for finances. Finally, there are home and family documents, including a temporary guardianship authorization, an authorization for foreign travel with a minor, a pet care agreement, a child care and elder care agreement, and an authorization for a minor's medical treatment.

Note that a healthcare directive is also known as a health care proxy or a durable power of attorney for health care. This essential document appoints someone to make medical decisions for you if you are in a health crisis during which you are incapable of making decisions for yourself. If you do not have one of these notarized and on file, in that situation medical caregivers will turn to your nearest blood relative or your legal spouse to make life-and-death medical decisions for you. It is recommended that you think long and hard as to who you would prefer to give that type of medical authorization to, because the ultimate decision is whether or not to "pull the plug."

A living will is slightly different than a healthcare directive. This legal document indicates what type of medical treatments you would or would not want if you were in a coma and unable to state your wishes. This is the document in which a "do not resususcitate" (DNR) order is usually contained if you are in a longterm coma, and/or if you wish life to be maintained by mechanical ventilation, artificial nutrition and hydration if your body is brain dead. Boilerplate language of a living will is frequently insufficiently specific to deal with these issues, using too many generic euphemisms. So feel free to edit both the healthcare directive and/or the living will so that your appointed proxy for healthcare is absolutely clear as to what you would desire him or her to decide on your behalf.

A terrific bonus of this software is the ability to do one, free, living trust. A living trust is also known as an "inter vivos" or "revocable" trust. It is a written, legal document through which your assets are placed into a trust while you are still alive. It is instantly transferred, without need for probate court (except if there is a pour-over will--see below), to your designated beneficiaries at your death by your chosen representative, who is known as a "successor trustee." In contrast, a will does not take effect until after you die and must go through probate court, with associated attorney and court fees.Though, to be honest, if you have a small estate and there is little chance of your will being contested, any associated court fees will be nominal and there will be little need for your executor to hire an attorney, whose fees would be paid out of your estate.

Even if you use this software to create a living trust, it is advisable to seek legal advice to take the steps associated with completing all that it involves, but your legal fees should be substantially smaller than the approximately $1500 it might cost if the attorney had to do all the work that is contained in filling out this form at the Nolo website. Examples of other steps required in completing an estate plan with a living trust include changing the beneficiary on your life insurance policy, IRA and 401(k) plan to the trust, transferring all your most valuable property into the trust (for example, your primary home, other real estate, stocks/bonds/mutual funds, money market accounts, brokerage accounts, jewelry and antiques, precious metals, works of art, valuable musical instruments, business assets, royalty contracts, patents and copyrights). It is also highly recommended to create a "pour-over will" that provides for the distribution of any assets that you have inadvertently excluded or acquired after you create your living trust and before the time of your death. Though in most states you can avoid probate entirely if you have a living trust without a pour-over will, if you have a pour-over will, just like any will, it is required to go through probate.

Be aware that this living-trust free gift has a time limit. You have one year to complete your living trust online on Nolo's website.

All in all, this user-friendly software is definitely worth the very reasonable price it costs to purchase it.

title: Love it!
text:
I got this app on my Kindle Fire so I could share quotes from Kindle books on Facebook, and I was delighted to discover that the Facebook display on the Fire, especially photos, is stunning! I actually like it as well or even better than the Facebook display on my laptop and desktop computers.

Best of all--this app is free!

title: Extremely convenient
text: I got this app on my Kindle Fire so I could share quotes from Kindle books on Twitter. It works really well for that purpose and, best of all, this app is free! Highly recommended!

title: Excellent app
text:
This is a very helpful little app that does a great deal considering it is free:

It can analyze the strength of your own wifi system.
It can analyze the strength of wifi systems around you.
It provides data on how well your network is operating.
It can tell you the strength of the signal of your network and others around you.
You can use it to figure out where there are dead zones in your wifi system and figure out more easily how to correct them.

The old maxim, "You get what you pay for," certainly isn't true with this excellent app.

title: Useful, free radio app
text:
I'm not a big fan of talk radio, which I presume is the main function of this app. (If it has another, it is not user friendly enough for someone with a casual approach to it like me to figure it out.) The main reason I got this app is because a friend recommended it to me to presumably use to listen to some cable-TV news shows I occasionally watch. Unfortunately for this purpose, I discovered that the selection for cable news, at least the ones I was searching for, is slim. Only one out of about 10 shows I might have potentially cared to listen to is offered.

Stitcher offers automatic recommendations to its users. However, though the recommendations it made to me made logical sense, I only ended up liking about 2% of the shows it recommended--not a very good average.

I like that the home page lists the day's headlines; if you click on them you get 30-second clips of each main news story. The app allows you to set up favorite stations, up to 10 categories--if you want more than 10 stations, you have to store them under one of your existing categories. There seems to be a working bookmark when one is listening to a podcast, but no ability to fast forward or rewind a show that I can find. Stitcher is also inconsistent on how many current and past shows it offers as podcasts of the few cable news shows it offers.

In short, rather than using Stitcher for cable news shows, I've had to instead access the Internet on my smart phone to go to the website for those shows to listen to videos of past shows.

In spite of my complaints, though, this app is certainly worth the price I paid for it--nothing.

title: A handy way to use your Netflix account
text:
I never thought I'd watch any movie or TV show on a screen as tiny as the one on my iPhone, but it's so simple and convenient to watch my favorite TV shows and movies this way, I find I don't mind the small screen. I just have to remember to bring along my iPhone ear plug so I don't disturb anyone around me.

Netflix runs well on my iPhone with no delays. When I call up the app, it goes right to my instant-viewing queue on Netflix so I just tap on whatever I've got near the top of my queue. It works the same as Netflix instant views online, staying cued where I stopped when I have to exit the app. I can also pause what I am watching by just tapping the screen to summon a pause button.

Very convenient and fun!

title: Wouldn't be without it
text: We've used TurboTax for our taxes for at least a decade. It has every software bell and whistle imaginable and many I would never have thought to ask for. This software has saved us untold thousands in tax-preparation fees over the years. It can be purchased as an instant download, but my husband always likes to get it as a disc which can be re-loaded on the computer in the future if a need for it ever comes up, such as (which sadly is all too common) the computer it was originally on crashes and burns and has been replaced.

title: One-stop shopping for the home office
text:
This is a fairly expensive piece of software, but it does a lot. It allows you to do word-processing, including letters, reports and newsletters. You can create complex spreadsheets that can be turned into charts and graphs for visual presentations. You can create slideshows, proposals and other interactive reports. You can create PDF files. You can manage your email, use online services to collaborate with people across the country and around the world, and you can store, collect and use information from almost any source imaginable. In short, this program works much like Microsoft Office and is compatible with it.
I highly recommend this suite of programs for people who, like me, have their own home business. It's one-stop shopping for almost anything you could need.

title: Excellent Functionality for Relatively Low Price
text:
This program is created by the makers of Corel Painter, which many consider to be the best painting and illustration software on the market. This software is a kind of Corel Painter Lite, and as such is a nice way to gain some initial exposure to digital art.

I'm not a particularly sophisticated software user myself, so I was happy to discover that this program is relatively easy to use. I installed it on my laptop, which has Vista, with few problems. The written manual is well done, and the video tutorials are especially helpful.

If you have even a mild interest in art, crafts or photography, this program will give you endless hours of pleasure. You can add special touches to your photos. You can create greeting cards. And if you are a fan of scrapbooking, you should see what you can do with it using this program!

I recommend this program to people of all ages, and it is especially fun for families to use together.

title: Fast delivery and good value.
text: Used Turbo Tax for many years and have not had any issues with the IRS. Fast delivery and good value.

title: Four Stars
text: Used Turbo Tax for years.  Price seemed to have increased this year, but still able to dive right in.

title: Issues with Windows 10 compatibility caused Online Updater not to ...
text: Issues with Windows 10 compatibility caused Online Updater not to work.  Had to spend several hours on the phone with two different people at TT to resolve a problem they eventually said was a known bug.  H&R Block next year for me.

title: Microsoft for the Mac
text:
This worked more or less OK to set up the MS Office Suite on a Macbook Air, and the program itself seems to work OK - at least as well as Windows features on an Apple machine can be expected to work. I have not had any crashes, anyway.

As far as setup goes this was kind of a pain, though pretty much my fault - it requires your Microsoft sign-in, which I'd done years back and never used again. Make sure to have that on hand when you begin set up and you will save yourself a lot of time. It would be nice to just enter a product key and get functionality, but that's not the way of the world these days...

I am a Windows user and set this up on my wife's Mac. She did not have any trouble with it but it was a steep learning curve for me as the scroll bar keeps disappearing on me, right clicking doesn't do what I want, and the program I was using (Word) wants to stay in Insert mode when I want to Edit text blocks. I am not sure if this the Mac aspect or new Word functionality - any Windows release is two steps forward, two steps backward, a diagonal leap in a surprise direction, and a pratfall or two. I use 2007 at work but it's nice to have the latest thing at home. Maybe!

title: FREE Commercials!

When this app works, it works great. I watched at least half of "Silent Hill" in crisp HD with no buffering or loss of definition. I've also seen the first fifteen minutes of "Marie Antoinette." The selection of offerings isn't huge, but hey - they're 'free.'

Unfortunately, I missed a bit and tried to 'rewind' - BIG mistake. Crash! Attempts to reload the movie proved futile - although the app had no problem hitting me with commercials every time I tried restarting, it couldn't seem to connect to the movie part of its site.

So if you don't mind iterating commercials, it's a great app. For film/tv viewing - not so much.

title: NBC News by NBC News Digital LLC
text:
NBC News by NBC News Digital LLC
I was on my Amazon Fire Box, watching TV when I noticed this app to just watch the NBC affiliate news with out using my Uverse service. I just thought I would try it and watched for a while when the coverage was endless of Exfirst Lady Nancy Reagan's death. This is a free app, so I won't be analyzing the way NBC covers events such as this, maybe I should do so here, but the app is why I am reviewing, not the endless coverage that barely deserves more than mentioning. The app is a very good feed, clean and HD picture with excellent sound delivery. The colors even seemed to be even brighter, so I checked by switching back and forth from the Uverse feed. It is a better quality delivery and I will use it more in the future. When I first looked when it came on, it seemed it would also cover more NBC affiliate feeds, but as yet I have not tried any other than the NBC News channel. I like it and this review may go up if there is more and better feeds included.

title: Weather HD for Tablets Free
text: I downloaded this app to my Kindle and I keep an Icon on my Home screen, available for use when I'm interested in seeing what the weather is like where my family lives, in different areas of the Country.  It was free and for that price and the weather in the background of my use of tablet, it is to my liking.  I only spent a minimal amount of time setting it up for about 10 different areas of the country and have removed those I don't want to look at, like New York.  The HD part did not play any role in my decision to download this app, but it is pretty good graphics and the content is useful.  I use several different Weather sources on the different Computers, tablets and phone I have and each has something to offer, but at this point I still prefer having the ability to call up maps with active content occasionally, such as temp maps, active radar and Storm and Lightning tracking available on other weather apps on my Computer.  I spend much more time at my computer, than on my Kindle Fire Tablet or my smart phone.  Preference is a personal thing and relative to how you use an app and how you have developed the proper use of it.

title: Microsoft Windows 8.1 - Full Version
text: I am moving to Microsoft Windows 8.1 - Full Version as eventually, it will become a necessity.  It does take some getting used to, 8.1 has removed mush of the buggy problems from the original issue of 8, so the learning curve won't be hampered by just getting it to work.  Here is the real story.  The moved from a first priority desktop delivered program, to a pad program, as we all feel, it will be the computer of the future.  I went ahead an got a blue tooth keyboard from Amazon Basics, and there you go, something you need to add on, like a desktop.  It does seem faster than my old Windows 7 and I've worked through the learning curve and actually work off my desktop as on the old 7.  The toggle back and for the from desktop to apps screen, which resembles the Pad screens, is a one click operation and not that hard to maneuver and learn to get used to.  If you have a learning disability, in that you hate changes you can stick with the old Windows 7 for your Desktop computer, but sooner or later it will go away. title: Good value
text:
First - I am running this on a 13 inch mid-2010 MacBook with a 2.4 GHz Intel Core 2 Duo, using OS 10.8.3.

Installation - It took me awhile to install this because I had to first upgrade my OS before I installed this. I did tie up my computer for an evening installing everything but most large installations do take a chunk of time so I did not think this was excessive. After upgrading my OS this software installed easily for me.

I have noticed that my computer has seemed to slow slightly but I have been having issues with my router and have an on-going problem with my internet provider so I am reluctant to attribute this solely to this software. In any case the slowing is not a huge problem at this point any way.

This is the first security software I have used since, as a confirmed Apple user I have always subscribed to the theory that only PCs have problems. This has been the case in the past but it appears as though Apple products are becoming victims of their own success. After one of the Apple Geniuses advised me that I had some malware and should get some security it was time to get some protection.

After installing this software I have noticed some of my e-mail have been labelled as suspected spam, certain attachments have been flagged and some sites I have tried to access have been blocked. Happily most of the e-mails were benign, as were the majority of the attachments. The e-mails, attachments, sites that I recognized I had no problem opening despite the warnings, the ones I didn't recognize I of course left deleted. This scanning has been particularly useful to me recently since I am in the final weeks of planning a wedding, and the number of e-mails, spam and otherwise that have been turning up in my mail is astounding.

I have discovered that this software package includes the antivirus software, and so for an additional couple of bucks this is a great value.

title: Seems to be working fine
text:
I am using a 13 inch MacBook (mid 2010) which has a 2.4 GHz Intel 2 duo core processor. In order to get this to run I needed to update my operating system to OS 10.8.3. After updating my OS getting this to download was not a particular problem, just rather time consuming. After doing the initial scans etc for existing viruses, (I seemed to be relatively clean), things have been running quite smoothly.

I have not noticed any appreciable slowing of my system since installing this. I have noticed a few e-mails and Facebook postings that I have later discovered were bogus, a couple of which I fell for. Since I have not seemed to have picked up any viruses I can only assume that this software is working.

The installation was easy enough even though we don't have a dedicated disc drive. A web download install would have been more convenient for us.
Setup was pretty straightforward. I fired up the software, started plugging the same numbers we had in the packet and the results we shown in real time. I didn't have all the info we needed and I was still able to advance and come back to fill in the blanks at a later time. Everything we needed was covered. Mortgage info, stock gains and losses, charitable contributions, professional expenses...etc..

Overall, it was pretty easy to use amd it saved us several hundred of dollars. I would explore this option again next year.

title: beginner friendly
text:
So, the last piece of video editing software that I used was adobe premiere. . . in 1996. I used that program to assemble my demo reel. So moving forward 23 year, I figured I'd give Pinnacle Studio 23 a test drive.

First off, the program arrived on disk. I installed onto a system without an optical drive. So a big deal, just be sure to get the non disk version if you're running a laptop without a disk drive. I installed it onto windows 10. That was simple and straight forward.

So as far as usage, much of the approach has not changed. Which is good. It's very simple idea to cut together footage and assemble them. The basics don't change. What does though, is the additional capabilities.

More recently, but still a while ago, I used Nuke to update my demo reels. So that was a compositing program. When I was going through Studio 23, I found many of those features adopted by the editor. It looks like there's been a lot of traction on getting some cross functionality.

So for someone who has not used a video editor in a while, this looks be a quite an evolution from just timeline clip editing to added full feature compositing and effects all rolled into a beginner friendly interface.

title: Q---k B****s Refugee
text:
This is better than the alternative. The learning curve is less steep, and there's a little less begware involved. All things considered, I like my multi-app workaround that I have pieced together over the years (one macro at a time) better, because upgrades are way less painful and my data files are never all in one possibly inaccessible one day basket. Putting myself through school as an executive assistant came with some reliable transferrable skills even as the technology has gotten better.

BUT, for people who don't have the time, patience, or inclination, this is the better of the big two. Set up is straightforward, and it doesn't start trying to upset you before you've entered all your data. Payroll is extra $, but most everything else you might need for a small business is included.

title: Beware the Upsell
text:
I haven't even had a chance to start using this, and it has already started begging for more money. I installed it on my computer right after I bought it. It found my bank but couldn't - for some unknown reason please try again later - download my data. Basically told me tough luck: export a csv from the bank and then try to import it. Well, great. I figured I'd get back to it when I had an extra day or so to figure it out.

Now, Quicken is sending me notices that my subscription is about to expire. WHAT subscription? It's been less than 60 days on what is supposed to be a 14 month product, and I did not sign up for ANYthing.

I stopped using Quickbooks years ago because it was little better than ransomware. I had hoped Quicken would be better. Apparently it's just as bad. If it had worked as expected, it would have been worth the money and not a penny more, It didn't, and it already has it's hand out. I wish I had never bought this.

title: Don't Buy the Pro Version Unless You Can See Your Files in the Free Version
text:
I bought the pro version for Mac and was able to recover about 70,000 files from a disk that no other recovery tools (Disk Warrior, TechTools) could see, let alone mount. The process was incredibly slow and tedious and occupied my computer for two days.

The free version does pretty much nothing except let you know whether it's even possible to get your data back. But I wish that I had started here instead of buying the other two (which I had to do in order to see whether they would work - they don't give refunds, either - hopefully their "maintenance" features will prove useful over time).

Suggestion: the software makers tell you to view any files the software manages to find before you pay for the pro version. If the files look like gibberish, you'll be wasting your money to try to recover them.

One more suggestion: stay away from Seagate backup drives. Stay away from Seagate drives, period. I had so many redundant copies of my photos. They were on Seagate internal drives, Seagate backup drives, and Seagate NAS drives. Any kind of drive can fail, but this was a total of five drives, all made between 2012 and 2016, all dead.

title: Reliable and Runs Better Than It Did in Previous Years
text:
Bottom line: any tax software, at best, is only as good as your numbers and your proof of your numbers, but if you have good documentation TurboTax is an excellent way to enter it, especially if you have questions along the way about how very tax laws apply to your situation.

I do my husband's taxes for his two sole proprietorships, each of which involves tons of small transactions - basically a couple of odd jobs every day - and I have been using this product pretty much since I started doing them. During that time, I developed some cognitive issues from lupus, which means that I need a lot of backup/double-checking to make sure I enter everything and my numbers are correct. It's stressful. I have to go really slowly. And I stopped using Quicken products many years ago for the books because, like I said, I have lupus, and Quicken got harder to use and did less and less that I needed it to do with each new version that cost more and more.

TurboTax Home and Business has gotten better, though. I use a really simple app from the AppStore called AndyTaxBooks - the interface is almost juvenile, it's so simple. I design my own categories and enter the numbers from the receipts and invoices over the course of the year, and if they match what I get from TurboTax when I enter them there, I know they are correct. There's no import into the tax software from my little app, but manual entry backed up with adding machine tapes stapled to each stack of paper in a category is the easiest way for me to check my own work (I know - I am old). It's also the easiest way for me to keep up with any tax law changes from one year to the next, which is one of the main reasons I use TurboTax. And the reason I say that it has gotten better is that even though I am on a Mac the program runs smoothly - in previous years, I would have to sit through numerous crashes and freezes, especially if I clicked on one of the question/more information buttons. This year it runs great.

title: NOAUTHZ
text:
After about a year of using this with no troubles, the app is now throwing constant NOAUTHZ errors. Customer service is nonexistent. I got an email acknowledging my help request and never heard from them again.

I'm seriously considering dropping the entire tier from my cable channel line up. I do not usually watch shows on my cable box, so if the app doesn't work, the subscription is useless.

text: We use this every year for my husband's proprietorships and honestly wouldn't change for anything. It is very thorough and easy to use. text:

If you're like me, and you watch these kinds of shows at bedtime, there is plenty here that is interesting enough to keep you from jumping to something else but not so interesting that it will keep you awake once you are ready to fall asleep.

There are also some really great obscure movies. Insomniac's dream.

Good free channel.

text:
This program is nowhere near as good as the versions for personal taxes and sole proprietorships. None of the help topics are helpful. There's no online assistance. And there was no E-filing option at the end. To add insult to injury, the state program was another sixty bucks and was similarly light on guidance.

I would have been better off filing out the forms by hand with a pencil.

title: Steep learning curve
text:
I very much like the new look and the touch emphasis in Windows 8. I also really like having a choice between viewing the widgets and viewing the traditional desktop page. I think, eventually, I will like Windows 8.

For now, however, I am experiencing a steep and extended learning curve that I am not really enjoying. I also didn't appreciate my Microsoft branded bluetooth peripherals going offline mid-installation (doesn't happen with a MacOS upgrade). This is 2013.

Apps are okaaaayyy. Youtube works better in a browser window than it does in the windows app. I guess the point of having it is so that your desktop and laptop and tablet can all look exactly the same, but I think that may not be the most efficient way for many people to organize things. Different tools for different jobs and all that.

title: Rescued from the BSoD
text:
It took a few tries for my laptop to pick up the media for some reason. I tried both UEFI and BIOS a couple times, and finally on the third BIOS try it picked it up.
I deleted my old windows partition and proceeded with the install. It picked up my product key on the hard drive so I didn't need to locate it some other way (good thing, scince it is not located on the computer anywhere). Now waiting on all the windows updates.
If you haven't created an install/boot disk or if you aren't confident in creating one, buy this. It works.
You do need to look up how to enter UEFI or BIOS for your computer (it's not standardized) but this information is easily searchable from your phone.
My only complaint is that the instructions included are limited to explaining that you need to boot from BIOS or UEFI and that you need to find those information on your own. It didn't explain how to do a fresh install after that. I found that information easily, but not from this seller.

title: A little challenging
text:
Given all the applications that I have had to learn over the years, I fairly familiar with how to learn and navigate through new applications. This Home design software compatible with Windows 11, 10, 8.1, 7 applications is challenging. It isn't as intuitive as I'd hoped. When I went to view the 2D dimensions, it was very small and it took me forever to figure out how to keep the focus when I zoomed in.

There are a lot tutorials available, but they are slow-paced so it requires patience.

Now, to be fair, it isn't that this application doesn't work. I was able to download and install in decent time. I can navigate through the menus. It is just that it does have a learning curve that I would compare to Photoshop. To translate, it can be learned but one has to have patience, so it does depend on the user. If a user is already familiar with this type of application, then it may be much easier.

title: Functions as expected
text: I've used TurboTax in the past. I used the PC version last year. The year prior, I used the Online. I actually really liked the Online version. Prior to that, I used another online software for about 3 years that I also liked. The point is, I'm fairly easy with tax software. As long as it has a good walk-thru for me to get all my information in, then I'm fine with it and so it is with TurboTax Deluxe 2019 Tax Software. It does what it needs to do. TurboTax software is still slow to open. It was the same with last year. I can that it has to check for updates but when it is within the hour, it gets a little annoying. Otherwise, it is good and functional program and it does make preparing taxes much easier. This version only has Federal. If one wants a State version, it is an additional cost.

title: Like it although it is a bit more than I need
text: Corel Painter Essentials 6 Digital Art Suite is as good as long as it is used. Like other graphic-arts-intense software, the more it is used, the easier it is to used. That's true of any software but I find it to be especially true of graphic arts software. We have a previous edition of Essentials which is used by my son who is the artist of the family. He likes it so I thought to gets this version. Probably what I like the most is the easy access to tutorials. For myself who is not an artist, it is still a little difficult to wrap my head around but the tutorials really help. My son has tried it out and he likes it. When he added his Wacom tablet, it was fun to watch him use the software. He definitely gets more out of it than I do. That point of that is that while it is fun and novelty-ish for me, it gets the most use out of someone who uses it for the features that it meant to be used for. I like it and as I learn more of it, it is becomes more interesting, but it is really meant for people who will use it for the tools it provides.

title: Some improvements
text:
I have a previous version of PaintShop for work so I wanted to see how the Corel PaintShop Pro 2018 Essentials version handles.

I don't see significant changes. The items listed in the "What's New" welcome area that are application functions are functions that I probably won't use. I do like the Welcome screen overall though. I do like the easy access to the video that demos the "NEW! Workspaces and Simplified UI" even if I'm not quite convinced that the workspaces and UI are actually simplified.

Overall, it is an application that I am comfortable using. The same things that I like in the version that I have been using is the same things that I like in the new version. At the same time, the things that I don't like are still in the new version. That said, there are some improvements that make it worth a long look.

title: Good quality information and ease of use
text: I have previous versions of NOLO Willmaker so I had some expectations for Nolo Quicken WillMaker Plus 2018 & Living Trust. I like this version a little better in that it seems to have more information while retaining the quality and ease of use that I have come to expect from the Nolo publishing line. This is a product that I would recommend for anyone to purchase. Even if one has a personal lawyer, it is good to have as a reference so that one has some knowledge of the process. Nolo does a great job of organizing the information so that it is understandable and makes sense.

title: Works on a troublesome laptop so I'm happy
text: This item came at the right time. The PowerDVD 14 that was pre-loaded on my laptop was glitchy with some of my DVDs (ex: sound and screen were going out of sync). After uninstalling and then loading up PowerDVD 16 Ultra, the DVDs that I had trouble are playing more consistently. Overall, I think it is my laptop that is sensitive so I don't blame the PowerDVD 14 that was preloaded, but the Power DVD 16 seems to be overcoming my laptop's sensitivities which is what I wanted it most for so I'm happy with it.

title: Good price and good product
text:
It took me awhile to go through "Corel VideoStudio Pro X4" but that was only because I was in between computers and my laptop could not run it.

That, however, was the only downside of this easy to use program.

Once I got my new PC, I was able to install it without problem and run the program. While I wouldn't call VideoStudio completely intuitive (for beginners), it isn't like Adobe's programs either. Once I got the hang of it, I was able to make some cute videos that were useful to me and at the reasonable price that this application sells, it works perfectly for me.

So to me, this is a good product and a good price and the user can actually use it without feeling like he or she needs to purchase 4 or 5 books to help learn how to use it.

title: Great software; less expensive than the online version
text:
After filing online with TurboTax for the past 8 years, I was looking for a discount. This software option was half (!!) as much as completing it online and was just as easy-to-use and thorough. The returns matched up perfectly. I've tried completing my own returns and without TurboTax, I miss hundreds of dollars of deduction-related tax savings. (Even at the higher price, it pays for itself. :)

I'm self-employed and have been watching the TurboTax online price creep up and up and up. After paying nearly $130 last year for federal & state taxes, I did some research on the best deal. Turns out this excellent software download is the way to go. I'm delighted it cut my costs in half.

The state filing is included . . . unless you want to submit electronically, then they want another $19.99. (I opted to print my state return and will enter it into the state system myself, or if it's too much bother, will just mail it in.) I had to file the federal return electronically first, then go back through the "review" section options to print just the state return. The system doesn't ask which you'd like to file electronically, so you have to "tell" it not to file your state taxes for the additional fee and then go back through and/or just print out the state portion of the file you save of your return.

title: and then asks you to pay them to make it less annoying. I really hate this type of game-development strategy
text:
It's a match-3 game with different setups that increase the difficulty of whatever your goal is in that setup, and then frequently with a move limit. And so when you get frustrated and lose, it asks you to pay money to get more lives/get extra helper doodads/get more moves. Basically, it annoys you, and then asks you to pay them to make it less annoying.

I really hate this type of game-development strategy. It's so sneaky. Why not just charge for a game once, when people buy said game, instead of making the game "free" but so annoying to play that they feel compelled to give you money to make it less annoying? Really? It's just such an underhanded way of getting people to pay for your product that it makes you, King games, seem like a bunch of conniving jerks. Stop doing this.

title: Seems like a good system
text:
I didn't go into this course in French cold. I studied that language in my youth, and wanted to brush up on it, so I signed on to Rosetta Stone. That was the first thing I liked about this course, I can study wherever I am and with whatever device I have with me. On my laptop, all I need to do is sign onto the site. On my phone, I open the app. And find out how much I've forgotten over the years. Yeah that was a shocker.

But it's all coming back now, with a mix of what seems pretty simply, and what is more challenging to me, even as a former student of French. I appreciate the scope of the lessons and their format. $209 gets you lifetime access to the lessons. For a modest amount you can purchase private tutoring and up your language game, making this a no-brainer for business users who have to become proficient quickly and stay in practice. It feels like a good deal to me.

title: I haven't been hacked, but I'm not sure why
text:
It's impossible to prove a negative, so saying that HMA has kept me from being hacked is a dodgy statement at best. It's true that as far as I know neither my phone or my computer has been compromised, but I can say that prior to using HMA that was true as well. One thing I do know is that it's caused a lot of browsing problems on my computer, so much so that I've uninstalled it as of today. Possibly it has something to do with using Chrome. I've seen other reviewers citing problems with that browser and HMA.

Some of the connections make sites block me when I'm using HMA, frex, I can't use a local connection because HMA reads as a bot to a couple of my regular websites, and they block me. I've worked around that on my phone, but never seemed to be able to do it consistently on my computer.

But because I can't prove that HMA has kept me safe, or even that it hasn't, I can't tell you if it's worth the price. I'm glad to have it on my phone, I do feel more secure using wi-fi away from home as long as HMA is running interference, and that's probably as good a rec as I can give.

title: Best version yet!
text:
ETA: Oh no no no no no. For the last month and a half upgrade ads have popped up on my computer screen every afternoon at 3:36 pm. I have tried everything I found online to stop them and no matter how many boxes I uncheck, the ads still keep showing up. If Corel wants to piss off customers, fine, but they've lost me. The program is good, but the advertising is much too intrusive and makes me want to uninstall the software. And if you don't want it on your computer, how worth the money is it?

I've been using PSP since it's original incarnation as a nice little image processing program for those of us who couldn't begin to afford Photoshop, and were willing to be satisfied with some basic tools. But over the years, PSP has become a serious contender, and now that Photoshop is subscription only (to which I say thanks, but no thanks) PaintShop Pro has really come into its own as a great stand-alone image program.

Yeah okay it's not the super deep professional quality you're getting with Photoshop, but how many of us need that? PSP offers solid editing tools at a very reasonable price, giving home users a lot of options, probably more than the average user will ever need. Opinions vary about the learning curve, and my guess is (based on my own experiences) that the more familiar you are with old versions, or with PShop, the easier you'll find learning to use PSP 2018.

title: Great value for the money
text:
It's really kind of hard for me to evaluate PSP X8 fairly because I find I'm always comparing it to Painter 2016. I use the latter a lot, the former... it simply doesn't do as much as either Painter, or Photoshop Effects. And that's fine, considering the vast difference in price points between it and the others. It's at the low end of the image manipulation range, and for that reason alone it deserves kudos, so let's talk about it from that point of view.

This is a program the average user is going to love having. It does a heck of a lot of cool stuff to your photos. There's a quick adjust tab which includes a lot of very basic fixes like noise removal, vibrancy, brightness and contrast and the like. The edit tab takes things a few steps further with resizing, photo effects, cropping, and even layers. It's a fairly sophisticated package for the home user, and if the effects aren't as finely tuned, as in a more expensive program, or the controls aren't as easy to master, it's really a small thing for the value you're getting

I can tell you that it works just fine in Windows 10 on my laptop, though it's a bit slow. Again, not a huge problem compared to the value you're getting. My best advice to you is to figure out what it is you'd want to use an image program for and buy accordingly. If you're simply not sure, then start with PSP and see if you strain its abilities. If so, you can always move up.

title: I'm liking this a lot more than I thought I would.
text:
I'm surprised at how much I like this program.

Having tried quite literally dozens of paint and photo programs since the early 80s, including, of course, Photoshop in half a dozen different iterations, the one drawback I've found with most of them is that they either deliver functionality or they're easy to use. Corel Painter 2016 is a remarkably functional program with an easy learning curve. Or at least I've found it so. Within a few minutes of booting it up for the first time, I produced the quick cat sketch below. It took me no more than about ten minutes to figure out how to use various brushes to good effect.

Using the Dynamic Speckles and various particle brushes was a little more difficult, but I got a handle on them pretty quickly and produced the landscape below in about half an hour. Yes, I know both images are rough; they aren't intended to be finished products, just examples of what you can do if you're willing to play a bit and explore the various controls. Only occasionally did I end up yelling at the program for some non-intuitive method of working that temporarily stumped me. (Yes, I do yell at programs. It helps.) Of course it helps that so much of the functionality adheres to Photoshop standards. Much of Painter should feel familiar to you.

Look, everyone who uses this is going to have a different response to this program based on their experience with other image programs. Since this is a chunk of change, I would say go to the Corel website and download the trial version before you lay out any money. Try Painter Essentials (another program I like) as well as Painter. Play with them and see how they feel. It doesn't matter how much functionality a program has if it feels clunky and awkward to you. If you do like the feel of it, as I do, then I really recommend that you take the financial hit and buy Painter. Essentials is good, but not nearly as good as the full version.

And have some fun! That's what this is about. You're getting this program to help you express yourself. If it's not fun, why bother doing it? And if you do use it, why not post a review with some of your work?

title: The best thought-out program for writers I've ever used
text:
I've spent years writing in Word. I've tried other programs for writers but until Scrivener came along, there was nothing that really worked the way I needed it to. I needed to make notes, I needed to keep reference photos, I needed to have separate folders for the story itself, and all the bits and pieces of information I'd collected about it. I needed a place to keep templates for character development and other aspects of the story. In short, I needed a place where I could keep my story, story bible, and all my resources in one place so that I could click back and forth between them in one program rather than hopping around from one program to another. Scrivener is that program.

I've begun to think of Scrivener as Photoshop for writers. You can do good work in it without even scratching the surface of what it can do. Honestly, I discover new functions in it all the time. I only recently discovered how to make comments on my text! The point here is that you need to keep at it. Do the tutorial, poke around in it and see what it does. Yes, there's a learning curve, but there is for virtually any stand-alone word processor worth using, too, and Scrivener does so much more than a word processor will.

I was lucky enough to be able to take advantage of a half-price offer given to NaNoWriMo winners, but I can tell you that now that I've used it for close to a year, I would happily pay full price for this program and still feel I got a bargain.

title: Definitely an improvement
text:
Maybe it's just me but Office applications seem to have become much more user friendly since I began using them umpty-gazillion years ago. The addition of Publisher and Access to this package is welcome, but to be honest, I'd prefer a more comprehensive publishing solution to be added to Office Pro. Why not add Front Page?

That said, the ease of both installation and maintenance has also improved with a quick download and a few minutes of installation during which you can actually use Office. The templates are simple to download and use, and each application has been made more intuitive, at least to my way of thinking.

With the install you get some Skydrive space with an option to buy more. I find the whole Skydrive thing a bit confusing, but I like it as a backup for my files. The free space is more than enough for me; I find it pretty generous.

All things considered, I think Microsoft's suite of Office applications has improved to the point where I'm not spending most of my time yelling at the computer while I work. That alone makes it worth the price.

title: A skeptic comes around
text:
Yes, I was. I was skeptical that any kind of software could begin to help me create legal documents for end-of-life situations. I found out how difficult that kind of thing is when I tried to help my parents through the maze of decisions and documents and choices. To be fair, their estate was somewhat complex, and mine is simple, but with laws varying by state, I wasn't sure that you could have anything like a one-size-fits-all solution.

Enodare has proved me wrong with Will Writer 2012, a surprisingly comprehensive application that helps create much more than just a will. It helps you through the writing of an estate plan, the forms needed for living wills, living trusts, powers of attorney for both healthcare and finance and of course the will itself. Possibly the most important of these is the estate planning part of the software because if you don't have a solid estate plan, nothing can really help you put together documents that will ensure that your wishes are honored at the end of your life. So after you've poked around all the other forms, sit down and spend some serious time with the estate planner. Trust me when I say that everything else will be easier if you complete it to your satisfaction.

Enodare provides forms which can be filled out to cover virtually any wishes you might have. Okay well, any wishes I might have. Your mileage may vary on this since it's one of the most personal things you'll ever do. And alongside each worksheet there's an explanation of what the questions mean, what the intention is behind them. If I have a quibble with this software at all it's that I didn't always find the explanations as clear or comprehensive as I would have liked. The program also provides notices of revocation of all of these documents, just in case you have second thoughts after you've created them.

Before you work with any of these forms you should give a lot of thought to how you want to dispose of your estate, and how you want your medical and financial situation handled if the time ever comes when you can't make decisions for yourself. Be sure you understand the laws in your area (There's a legal manual included.) and if you have any questions you can't answer easily, then it's time to consult a lawyer. If you have a complicated estate, you might want to put these papers together and take them to a lawyer to find out if the documents will hold up. Will Writer can be a terrific help but it won't handle every situation. It's still not a one-size-fits-all solution, but it's a darn good start if you're willing to do your homework.

Oh... and the reviewer who called it a knockoff of Microsoft Word? Not so much. I really have NO idea what he's talking about.

title: Startlingly Good
text:
Long-time Photoshop user here, and I have to tell you that I am impressed with how far Corel has brought its suite of graphics software in the few years since I've used it. In spite of it being a suite, it isn't bloated with a lot of specialized applications. You get a good, solid image handling program, a vector-based drawing program and a screen cap one. No website builders, 3D stuff or video applications which are likely to take up a ton of space on your hard drive and never be used.

In many ways I find the drawing program better than anything I've seen from Adobe. The controls are far more intuitive. And in fact, since one of the ways I judge how well a program performs is by how easy it is to get it up and running at a basic level without reading any of the manuals, I'd have to say that in many ways the whole suite is as good or better than Adobe's offerings in the intuition department. If you can handle Photoshop, you can certainly learn Corel fairly quickly.

There's a lot I could say, but I'd prefer to recommend that you check the program info on this page and see if it sounds like something you can use. I don't think you'll be disappointed in the quality, nor do I believe you'll be defeated by the learning curve. I think you'll find this suite well worth having either on its own or as an addition to the graphics programs you already use.

title: It does what I need it to do.  Mostly
text:
A year or so ago, I switched to Outlook, and while I can't say that I really liked it, I did find that it was an improvement over my old email program in a couple of important ways. It offered integrated address book, and calendar functions, and it didn't crash every fifteen minutes. I got used to its drawbacks because it offered me greater functionality.

Along comes Outlook 2007 with the contact manager, and suddenly there's a whole new level of functionality to make me even happier. Now I can sync my Outlook calendar with my Google calendar, import RSS feeds and, with a tiny app and a couple of keystrokes, I can import contact information quickly from any online resource.

On the downside, and predictably, it takes forever to install. Backup of everything except archived mail is sort of a pain, and the way it creates files is kind of squirrely. But in general I'd say that it's made keeping track of my life just a bit simpler. And that's worth a lot to me.
