title: Super performer, incredible really
text:
I had done a pre-market review when Dragon 15 Legal first came out. Those initial comments are completely overcome by the revisions.

Dragon 15 legal is machine language dictation at the highest levels. It takes some number of thousands of words dictated to establish your 'profile' and then you're off to the races. The software is like correctly anticipating my next words.

The command structure is huge. There a books to help. I'm no master of command. I dictate into word where Grammerly supports higher level sentence and paragraph structures. I'm using a cardiod mic from Andrea Communications NC-181VM USB mic available on Amazon.

I mostly dictate 3-5 page briefs. I've finished several with no word errors. That's what I call performance.

title: Dictation is unnatural
text:
Writing and public speaking is the way I make a living these days. Dictation has always been unnatural for me. I wish it was easier done than said but it’s not. My productivity has most definitely suffered because dictation is simply an unnatural process.

However, I have stayed close to the tech. Dragon by demonstrated performance lead the tech race. I’ve bought at least 4 earlier iterations of Dragon and given the pressure to get more words on paper faster I’m back again with Dragon 15.

What’s perhaps ‘different’ this time is that Dragon is a clearly aligned as a step in the bigger process. Its purpose is to get words on paper, a first draft perhaps unlike I’m used to using and independent of the MSWord processor for formatting, punctuation etc. I used to Dictate into Word and spend too much time in sequence with the Word processing.

Now, the ‘first draft’ is the rather pure Dragon objective. Editing and formatting are independent processes.

So my first impression of Dragon 15 is it’s paradigm shift not for the tech but with my perception of the ‘first draft’ paradigm shift. Dragon 15 + quality ($150) cardioid mic with off/on switch + your voice baseline is quit expert at converting voice to text and ramblings into coherence.

I’m thinking the nooks and crannies of the software is the key to the fine tuning. I rambled off about a 5000-word narrative and began to ‘get it’.

I’ve used it on a couple e-mails and imagine that the dictation + MSWord was no slower than writing. That’s an important first step for me. Is it worth the effort? I think after watching the tech for 20 years that it is. It’s not the tech, it’s me.

title: significant upgrade
text: Big improvement over previous Kindle app. Timely and extensive news for on the go.

title: Works fine ...
text: I'm happy

title: Tiny improvements make the evolution fun to watch
text:
I've used TurboTax for decades to keep the CPA honest and more specifically to understand what the CPA tells me. My taxes are complex because the regs and laws are like hyper-complex as I have to consolidate `S' corp entities.

So my CPA sent the final package to me this week and I compare it to the TurboTax 2011 that I've scratch-papered while the accountants work away. A couple entities don't use Intuit based accounting. I have to manually transpose the #'s to TurboTax. For the QuickBook accounted entities, it's like magic to import into TurboTax.

2011 was a complex year as I plumbed the `loss' side of the tax code. I have a good mental image of the code as it applies to `profits'. I've never had a loss to account for. I expected to find a difference in the `quality' of TurboTax's underpinnings for the increased number of forms that accompany `loss' reporting (it costs CPA $ to account for a `loss' ... indeed an irony if there ever was)

The score between TurboTax and the high $$$ CPA? To $mall to mention. TurboTax adroitly managed the correct new forms. If I didn't worry about IRS and state biz audits and wanting someone sitting beside me, I'd not pay the outrageous CPA fee.

The folks at TuboTax are masters of discovering tiny ways to improve the product. The TurboTax price goes down year over year while the IRS complexity goes up. You can learn a lot using TurboTax yourself. Such a deal! ... 5-stars.

update 3/20 ... I don't get the download problem reviewers are citing. This particular product version I'm reviewing is the hard disk and not the download version. I just checked again. There can't be any confusion. Amazon says only 10 left at this moment. Is Amazon combining reviews?

title: As advertised! ... but, there is a download glitch with an easy fix.
text:
Great price, great product, great service ... but, I've been using Turbotax for a hundred years

Amazon had far and away the best price today. This is the first year that I've seen Turbo-tax widely discounted. I use Turbotax Biz to 'what-if' front run my CPA. Turbotax S-corp biz tax prep is frankly as good as the CPA.

There is a download issue that Amazon could easily fix to satisfy the 1-star reviewers. The first 'download' button sends you off into cyberspace and froze up my browser. The freeze had the characteristic of a download management system malfunction. My second try was successful BUT I used a 2 step process: First, follow the links to Amazon's download management software and download the Amazon engine first. Second, download Turbotax. After the Amazon downloader was installed, Turbotax self-loaded in 2 minutes.

title: home/office biz disc=2 computers, student/home disc = 3 computers
text:
Office 2010 is 5 stars. The activation & pricing scheme is 1 star. New average = 3 stars.

Office 2010 is different from previous MS office iterations in its marketing scheme.

Home/student disc = NO OUTLOOK, key good for 3 computers

Home/Biz disc = OUTLOOK, key good for 2 computers

Home/Biz activation key = One computer

Very sneaky this change up in my opinion to catch buyers off guard. Read carefully. Learn from my lesson, I bought home student (3 computers) FIRST and discovered after purchase that Outlook wasn't there. I wasted $150. I'd normally suck it up in my rating as it was my error ... the package clearly DOES NOT mention Outlook. MSOneNote is new and an unknown for now. I don't see an intuitively obvious use for it yet. As a trade off for Outlook, it seems like a loser.

The second buy was home/biz ... except, to find it is only good for 2 computers ... yet another surprise AND I was more careful in reading fine print this time.

The product description needs to provide this information upfront regarding both no Outlook in Home/School and the # of computers/key in the same bold font as the MS attributes. I called MS ... I can generally cajole my way to an extra add on, but not this time. MS offered no consideration for my home/student wasted $150 which the MS activation center said was clearly obvious with overlapping licenses for Word, Powerpoint & Excel.

I have no gripe on the price. It's worth buying but the buying experience is not happy for multiple computers. The Amazon price is the lowest available.

MSO 2010 did solve a quirky Outlook 2007 behavior that took up residence on one of my 4 home machines that had rendered it non-email friendly. I must have spent close to $1000 in tech support to unsuccessfully resolve that one.

title: Backup your company file first, and be prepared to call the Philippines if renewing payroll
text:
I've been using QuickBooks for about 12 years now, since I got my first employee, and their payroll functionality has made doing payroll and filing taxes, and staying legally compliant, much easier, though I still deeply value the help of my bookkeeping guru and tax person.

I had been using QuickBooks Pro 2019 with enhanced payroll, so this was a simple upgrade, and I really appreciated that it transferred all of my preferences over for a very seamless transition.

Luckily it occurred to me to backup my file rather than just trust everything would convert over, because I had a few nailbiting moments where it refused to recognize my normal QuickBooks file. Luckily, it was able to restore my company from that backup that I did right before upgrading, and all was well. I would definitely, definitely advise that you backup, possibly in a few formats, prior to your upgrade just in case anything goes wrong.

The one thing that I absolutely hate about the upgrade every single year is that if you are purchasing QuickBooks payroll through Amazon or another retailer rather than letting QuickBooks auto renew, you have to actually call their customer service staff in the Philippines and have them manually generate a new number for you to make the payroll function. This is an absolutely useless and somewhat abusive waste of time, and over the years, having been forced into this half-hour phone call every year for 10 out of 12 years,

title: I'm starting to love QuickBooks again, great reporting for those in landscaping or construction
text:
About a year ago, I complained to a friend that QuickBooks Pro just didn't have the reporting features and types of charts that I would've really like to see, as a more visual person who owns a landscaping company. Well, I guess the problem is that I didn't have the right QuickBooks! Desktop premier 2019 is very similar to QuickBooks Pro 2008, aside from the extra features. I'm still learning how to use all of the different reports and options, but what I found is that things are now laid out in a way that is so much easier for me to understand as a visual learner.

I also didn't experience any bugs, crashes, or problems during the upgrade or since then with sending my file back and forth to my bookkeeper as a portable file. Overall this works exactly how I would expect, and has filled the gaps that I thought were present in the product when I was using QuickBooks Pro. I would definitely recommend this for anyone in the construction related or landscaping industry.

title: What an upgrade over 13!
text:
After using 13 for many years, and hearing terrible things about crashing with version 14 that discouraged me from paying the money to upgrade, I was delighted to test out Dragon naturally speaking 15.

I invested in a book about using Dragon NaturallySpeaking successfully, of which there are many available as inexpensive e-books here on Amazon. I followed the advice in one of those books about being very careful about microphone set up, testing the background noise with audacity to make sure my microphone wasn't too noisy and to adjust accordingly, and I declined Dragon's requests to evaluate my documents and emails, because I didn't want it to learn weird capitalizations or someone else's writing style (when it evaluates emails it is also looking at other people's writing style, vocabulary, and mistakes).

What I hated most about Dragon 13 is how it crashed constantly when switching from two different Word documents. I also hated that it would consistently capitalized things that I did not want capitalized, such as Brown, or Rose, and other words that could potentially be a proper noun, but usually aren't. I also hated that it would switch around in a Word document and constantly put the cursor someplace I didn't want, especially after manually making a correction.

Prior to upgrading, I read the Dragon 15 absolutely needed to run Word 2000 16 in order not to crash. So as part of my upgrade, I also upgraded my office suite, which is not my favorite way to spend money since it was otherwise still functional, but that is the program that I prefer to use with Dragon, so I did.

Between the two upgrades, I have had zero crash issues, and zero issues of Dragon putting the cursor in some random location. It still does tend to use capitalizations on words when not wanted, but I can work with that.

Overall, this was an exceptionally worthy upgrade and I am really glad that I upgraded to Word 2000 16 at the same time. I have narrated this entire review with zero corrections so that you can see what Dragon naturally does with text.

title: You'll need to upgrade to office 2016, great upgrade, Bluetooth headset is a throwaway
text:
I was having issues with the previous version of Dragon because it kept crashing when I would move my cursor around in Word 2013, and even when used in chrome or a more generic word processing app, sometimes the cursor would skip around in a way that was not necessarily easy to predict, and even when I manually place the cursor where I wanted it, sometimes it would just skip randomly to the middle of some word and start dictating there, which was deeply irritating and made my workflow a lot slower.

Reading reviews of this prior to installing, I realize that I was going to need to invest in office 2016 in order to use Dragon Professional Individual 15 effectively. After making that investment, I have had trouble-free dictating for two weeks now, even when skipping around a document or editing three documents side-by-side as I so often do in my work.

It works flawlessly in chrome, and I have had zero issues editing, correcting, or with random cursor movements.

The Bluetooth headset didn't work well for me to get accurate dictation results, and I personally went right back to using my wired blue snowball microphone instead after testing only briefly.

I would recommend that you pick up one of the many self published guides on Amazon to dictating, such as Taming the Dragon or its ilk, which provide a lot of small tips for using dictation most effectively and setting up your profile from the start. I have been dictating since Dragon first came out very very many years ago, since as a teen, I did not want to learn how to type. I read two of these self published manuals and even with all of my previous experience with dictation, I learned a couple of helpful tips that I do believe have increased my accuracy.

I would definitely suggest not allowing Dragon to evaluate your documents and emails, because in the past that introduced a lot of random issues such as misspellings, random capitalizations, and style issues from reading people's replies to my emails. If you want to very carefully create one document that has some of your specific vocabulary in it, that's one thing, but I would suggest slowly introducing your lexicon to the Dragon so it doesn't spit out a bunch of random stuff.

Overall, this was an extremely worthy upgrade from the last version of Dragon and I am very happy to have it. Get the version without the Bluetooth headset and save your money for a decent microphone, preferably a wired one unless you seriously need wireless, you'll have a lot clearer dictation results from a wired USB mic.

title: Make sure you have the premium version in order to upgrade, it won't work with home edition
text: Make sure that you have Dragon NaturallySpeaking premium 11 or 12, not the home edition. I realized when I was trying to install it that I was upgrading from a home edition, and it wouldn't let me. I'm lucky Amazon is allowing me to return it.

title: Hard to learn but seems to have some robust features
text:
After struggling to use Corel's Video Studio Pro software for years, and finally giving up on it after I realized it was running in the background and taking up a great deal of memory and causing errors, I was delighted to get to test this Sony movie studio suite. I'm especially excited, because I just got a Sony video camera, so I was thrilled to think that there would be no codec or compatibility issues when editing the videos from my new camera.

Sony obviously tried hard with their software to blend robust capabilities with ease-of-use. Unfortunately, I do not find this easy to use at all. While my videos were easy to import and I found it simple to trim the start and end point of the videos, I was unable to figure out how to lower the volume on one of my multiple audio streams.

I tried using the tutorial software, and while I love the simple way that they took me step-by-step through actually accomplishing the tasks available to show me, unfortunately they told me to click on some menu items that I simply could not find. For example, they told me to click on the edit menu and find a button to split my video. However, that option simply wasn't there. Luckily, they gave me a keyboard shortcut in the tutorial as well, so I just used that. While the tutorials were helpful in that they walked me step-by-step through each process, they could have simplified the learning process more by showing where the menu was that I was supposed to use war where the item was on the menu.

Also, the things that they chose to give a tutorial about, weren't really things that I cared to learn. At least not right away. How to configure a VST audio effect, how to create an audio cross fade, how to reverse an event... I don't even know what that stuff means. How about telling me how to turn the volume down on one of my audio streams?

That said, I do have faith that with time and a great deal of patience, I will be able to learn to adjust my home movies and put them together into a single movies showcasing a particular month in my baby's life, for example. However, I wish they had put a little less effort into coming up with weird effects such as making the color pale or blurry, and more effort into making some of the simple tasks that people will wish to do obvious and easy to understand.

title: I created a will in only 20 minutes - fast and intuitive to use
text:
After having a lawyer review my current will, which I wrote myself over the course of an hour, I wasn't surprised to learn that my current will was about as useful as a piece of toilet paper. While I don't have a very complex estate, just a home, a small landscaping business, a truck, and assorted household items, I really didn't want there to be any confusion about who received my assets in the case of my death. It would be tragic and completely unfair for my family to struggle to receive the assets that I have worked so hard to build to support us all.

While I'm only 34, it's never too early to think about these things. I know people who have died from sudden or prolonged illness, car accidents, and in other ways, and I felt like the short amount of time it takes to create a will and get it notarized was absolutely worth it for the peace of mind of knowing that my family would benefit from all of my hard work in the case of my death.

So I was delighted to test out this will maker plus software from Quicken, which installed in seconds on my computer and helped me create a will in less than 20 minutes. All I need to do now is take it to get it notarized and make sure that everybody involved has copies of it.

The software was incredibly easy to use, and lets you go step-by-step through the process. You can save your progress at any time and come back easily to change or update your answers. Also, in each section there are a number of resources included so that if you have a question, you can click for more information. This was incredibly helpful in learning how to specify the property that I wanted given to my beneficiaries.

There doesn't seem to be any limit on how many people can create a well using the software. I'm excited about this, because it means that I can help my whole family create wills, and avoid adding unnecessary stress to the process of dealing with our (humble) assets in the case of someone's death. I mean, it's horrible and hard enough when somebody dies, without having to deal with a bureaucratic mess as well.

This guide includes a free copy of The Legal Answer Book for Families, a 220 page book with a number of answers to common questions that people have with regards to their families. While this book isn't really targeted for people who are making wills, it answers a number of questions that are helpful for families such as laws regarding teenagers, child custody, elder care, marriage and name changes, tax breaks, divorce, adoption, and domestic partnerships.

Overall, this is a wonderful time saving piece of software and was extremely intuitive and non-frustrating to use. I couldn't believe that I was able to create an actual, legally binding will in 20 minutes and I didn't have to bang my head against the table or consult a professional in the process. Don't forget that you'll still need to take your will to be notarized before it is legally binding.

title: Eyestrain galore. Stick with your old version as long as you can.
text:
I have been using QuickBooks since 2008 and overall have had mixed feelings about the experience since then. While I love that overall this is a functional piece of software that lets me manage my employees and payroll as well as do normal business functions like invoicing and taxes, I hate how bloated the organization is. The help manual is decidedly unhelpful and ignores a number of simple questions. Then, when you call support, it takes forever and the representatives are generally rude and don't speak English very well. Worse, their company policies are unfriendly and it's like they know they have a monopoly on the market as most tax pros use QuickBooks, so small businesses like mine are forced into it unless we want to go it alone.

I upgraded to QuickBooks 2013 because Amazon provided a free copy as part of their Vine program to top reviewers. QuickBooks has a forced obsolescence and so since I was running 2011, I knew that I would be forced to upgrade by 2014 or else not be allowed to continue running the payroll software. This is why I had upgraded from 2008 to 2011 though 2008 had been working perfectly well. While the upgrade from 2008 to 2011 went surprisingly smoothly, importing all of my settings and preferences with no drama, this latest upgrade from 2011 to 2013 was a mess.

While it imported all of my company information and many of my settings such as the logo that goes on my invoices, 2013 changed the look of the user interface to make it much, much harder to read and impossible to personalize.

I am 34 years old with reasonably good vision, and after five hours of using QuickBooks yesterday, I retired for a nap with a headache and eyestrain. The new interface has a monochrome gray on gray look which makes it exceptionally hard to read the text. A lot of the text is a light gray on a charcoal gray background, which is notoriously hard for people to read. In addition, the font size looks much smaller than on previous versions. Looking through the help section, I found many complaints in their online forum about this new look, and found that unlike in previous versions where you can customize the look, in this new version QuickBooks has decided that we are not allowed to make any changes in how it looks. In fact, if I want to enlarge the text, I need to change the screen resolution on my monitor. I don't need to change the screen resolution on my monitor as everything else is just the right size, it's QuickBooks that is the problem!

Then, the software seems less stable than 2011. I've had it running for only a few days and it has already crashed! I don't think 2011 crashed on me more than once or twice over a course of years, so the fact that this new version has crashed in the first few days is very alarming.

Lastly, I haven't yet figured out if I can input the new payroll code without invalidating my previous payroll subscription. The last time I purchased the enhanced payroll in July of 2012, I had to call support to have them input the code, and they said that as soon as the new code was input, the previous code would be invalidated. That means since I called in two weeks prior to my previous subscription ending, I essentially lost two weeks of payroll subscription that I had paid for. The customer service rep I spoke to at that time said that there was nothing he could do besides advise me to call back the day before expiration to have them input the code, but given that I had been on the phone for 45 minutes already, most of that waiting for a rep, I wasn't keen to waste another 45 minutes to save a few bucks.

The problem here is that this software bundle that comes with QuickBooks enhanced payroll says that you must begin your payroll registration within three months of installing QuickBooks 2013. Does that mean that the payroll subscription I purchased back in July will be invalidated as soon as I enter in the new code? I'm planning on calling QuickBooks shortly to find out for sure, and I will update this review as soon as I learn more.

I am extremely unhappy with the new QuickBooks and would advise that if you possibly can, don't upgrade to 2013. Perhaps by 2014 they will have sorted out these issues and this will be a workable piece of software again. Until then, I'm undecided as to whether I'm going back to 2011 or not. I guess I will see if the eyestrain subsides.

title: Shocked at how well this works
text:
I first used Dragon NaturallySpeaking about 16 years ago when I was a teenager who did not yet know how to type. While it provided many opportunities for puns and malaprops, overall it was almost more effort to use Dragon then it was to type. Fast-forward 16 years, and Dragon has made huge strides in its voice recognition. After reading some reviews that were critical, I was unsure of whether I would find Dragon frustrating or a lifesaver. But after spending only 15 minutes training the software, I have found myself able to narrate blog posts, book reviews, and simple emails with few mistakes. I actually make more mistakes when typing then Dragon has been making through voice recognition.

Because I write sometimes 30 to 40 hours per week, issues such as carpal tunnel syndrome are always at the forefront of my mind. If I were ever to have an issue that would make it impossible for me to type, my income would drop precipitously. Realizing that voice recognition software has come so far over the years is a huge relief on my mind, and I've also been finding it a real pleasure to examine how I think differently when narrating as opposed to typing. It is almost like I'm using different parts of my brain, and my writing has a slightly different personality when narrating than when typing the normal way.

While I haven't yet explored all of dragons voice commands for navigating around my computer, I have found that for doing simple tasks that require writing more than one sentence, I'm really gravitating towards using software rather than typing. This may be because my keyboard is so worn out that a few of my vowels are no longer visible, so the hunt and peck method that I usually employ (I know that's terrible for a writer) is being less successful at the moment. But really, I'm finding it such a fun novelty to narrate rather than type simple reviews and blog posts that I'm really wishing I had purchased Dragon sooner.

Overall, if you've been on the fence about picking up Dragon NaturallySpeaking, I would definitely recommend it as seems like they have made enough changes to the software over the years that this latest addition is truly functional and useful and is really about as good as I could have possibly hoped. I narrated this review and the only changes I made were the mistakes that I personally introduced into the text, and adding parentheses above, which Dragon did not successfully insert. I expect that with more training time, dragons performance will only improve.

title: It's pretty good, but lots of bugs
text:
I didn't feel as though I had any choice in buying this software. I don't see a lot of other programs that offer the same capabilities in bookkeeping and payroll. But I can't say I am all that thrilled with it. In particular, I keep getting weird pop up windows saying windows is compacting the message store, whatever that means. There are a few other weird bugs too, like it crashes when trying to save as PDF or export to email.

It does what it is supposed to, but the learning curve is such that I have needed a lot of training on the program with my accountant, even though I am proficient at using many programs and have built my own websites. The support isn't the best, either.

So I give it a lukewarm 4 stars. I'd certainly buy it again, but if there was an alternative that did the same things - I'd buy that instead. I don't like all these ridiculous bugs.

title: An Investment that Needs Commitment
text:
I've been learning French since 8th grade. I was a better than I am now, but I never really mastered anything despite 5 years of French in school and trying to keep it up with various programs (usually cheap or free). Everyone knows about Rosetta Stone and everyone know hows how pricey it is. This is probably the reason why most people hesitate to purchase the program especially since there is no guarantee that it helps. I am two weeks into Rosetta Stone: Learn a Language with Lifetime Access (French) and I would have to say I am very pleased by the program.

I like how the program has a break down of mini lessons within the lessons. These mini lessons range from pronunciation, grammar, writing, vocabulary, and a core lesson. You will need a mic on your computer to complete these lessons. After you go thru each mini lesson you pass the lesson as a whole and go on to the next level. Once you pass level 2 you can play games and other extended learning challenges to help with your learning of the language. You also have access to private lessons if you choose to pay for them. Everything is thru the Rosetta Stone website so there is no software to download or worry about updates. Beware: This is for only one language! You cannot switch, so be careful when choosing you language. I read another review where someone was stuck with Swedish because their finger accidentally hit the wrong button. Don't be that guy.

What I like the most are the pronunciation lessons. When I first started, I thought I would be lazy with my pronunciation because I thought the computer wouldn't tell. After a while, I started failing challenges because I was completely off in proper pronunciation. So, the computer will get you! What I don't like the most is that I am basically starting from day one French and I already have the basics down. So, I'm kinda bored right now as I have to go through everything before I get to where I really am. Duolingo has an option to "test out" of certain lessons and I wish that was the case here. Since this is lifetime, I'm assuming that I will just keep progressing until I master French or at least that's the general idea. I'd like to just get to it since I've hand 5 year of formal French study, I'm sure I could pass a lot of the basics and get back into the areas that always gave me trouble.

Naturally, you will not really learn a language unless you are practicing everyday or nearly everyday, so this is really an investment due to the price and time that requires a commitment. If you are a newbie, this will work well for you. You'll be confused at first because this doesn't teach or show basics like the alphabet, sounds, and greetings like a French 1 high school class, but rather this teaches you the way children actually start to learn to talk thru seeing and hearing rather than through instruction. If you are someone who was partially proficient, this will frustrate you, but I am trusting in the process. You already know the basics, so getting back into just thinking about that language again has been a joy.

I have not used the app on my phone. It's not really something I would do away from home anyway. Make sure you check compatibility with your device. Lately in general, I've been having trouble with a lot of apps because my iPhone 5 will not accept newer apps since Apple decided to stop updating the iOS for the 5. So, just check the compatibility with your phone if you decide to get this to use with your phone or tablet.

title: Best self-guided program available!
text:
Having used Rosetta before, I can say this is the best way outside of a classroom (and better than some classrooms, depending on the method of instruction) to learn a language. It is fun and simple, and best of all, cuts translation out of the process so your brain goes straight from the concept to the correct word using images and games.
The problem with many simpler programs is their underlying premise that learning a language means translating words. That adds an extra step in your mind where you might think “book” and then come up with “livre” instead of just thinking “livre”. By never using a language other than the target one, you begin the habit of associating the concept with the target language word directly.
After doing this with simple objects for a short while, you begin to move on to Very basic phrases or connected ideas, increasing in complexity and building until you realize you are learning grammar and structure. Again, this is done very organically, which generally means less painfully! Haha
I’ve learned a few languages (I speak Spanish, Portuguese, and some French) in many settings and at many places: Spanish at home and in middle & high school, as well as a year of French; one year of French and one semester of Arabic at Yale; French brush-up later at a community college, where I learned Portuguese with an awesome teacher; and finally German online at that same college, where I learned nothing. I’ve had experiences ranging from Excellent teachers and programs all the way to utterly failed ones.
Another element I appreciate here is the fact the program cares about your accent! Oftentimes, students neglect this and sound awful, and in a full class, teachers can only do so much to help. Seeing visually roughly where you are and being able to play it back really can help.
Basically, if you are disciplined and want to learn a language, but you don’t have time to enroll in an in-person program, this is the Best program. Not Duolingo, not online courses at a college—Rosetta Stone! I’ve done all the other options, and so far, this is the best one by a mile!
Bon chance, mes amis!

title: steep learning curve, crashes on my Mac
text:
This review is for people like me who want their software to be intuitive, sleek, and cooperative; the sort of thing you can open up and start right away. If you are like me, then you will NOT like this software.

The installation is very simple and easy, with an option to do a digital download. This is very helpful if you have a newer computer like mine, which does not have a CD drive (newer MacBook Pro). That is the last straightforward thing, aside from the simple drag and drop import option.

This product suffers from too many options. The interface is somewhat overwhelming, and it makes it a little complicated to figure out where to start. After flipping through what look like good and detailed options I am not interested in, I check the filters and presets. They are comparable to many you might find in other products and are equally easy to use.

Going into some more specific items like the counterpart to "distort/liquify" in Photoshop, it was not cooperative at all. It didn't quite work for me. Then it crashed.

I have the newest OS for Mac, so crashing was not something I would have expected.

I tried for around an hour and decided to put it away. For those of you that don't mind watching tutorials and reading books about how to use software, my guess is that this will be fine, but as I said above, if you are like me and want it to be straightforward and easy to run, you will not like this program.

There is only one feature I intend to use that makes this worthwhile: watermark creator. I generally like to work with the subscription-based picmonkey, which is SUPER easy and intuitive. Occasionally, I also use photoshop. Anyhow, I designed a watermark on picmonkey, imported it to this program, and now it is Very easy to slap on that watermark automatically using presets. The automatic features of this software are good, and if you need to get certain things done en masse, this is an ok option. Other than that, I recommend picmonkey. It is around $33 per year and is QUICK. The downside to that is that you can't save layers, but whatever. It's still good for most things and is quick and easy.

title: Solid Suite
text: This is a solid suite that is easy to use. The interface, while somewhat dated in appearance, is simple. Installation was hassle free. I prefer WordPerfect to the more familiar brand. It has been around forever and works well. Quatro pro is a spreadsheet master. But I prefer Power point to presentations, probably because I am more familiar with it.Good option.

title: Tried and True
text: Word perfect is tried and true. It is reliable. It hasn't changed much. Easy to use the interface is simple if a tad dated. The word processing covers pretty much everything you need. The legal tools are helpful. Their version of power point is okay. I am more familiar with Powerpoint but this is not hard to master. Quatro Pro is an efficient way to produce and handle spreadsheets. If is fast. There is no email but that is not a deal breaker. This is a goid suite that has withstood the test of time.

title: If Word Processing is Your Priority
text: This suite can't be beat for word processing and formatting. Set up is quick and easy and it is straightforward to use. I am using it in my office where the emphasis is in document preparation and revision. This functions almost like a desktop publisher. However we do not do much group editing, an area where Word is better. Quatro pro, their spreadsheet program is on a par with Excel. Presentations works but it is not as impressive as Powerpoint. I tend to move between word, wordperfect and libre. For my purposes which emphasizes word processing, this program has the edge.

title: A Way with a Will
text: Even if your assets are limited it is important to have a will or trust. It provides a modicum of protection for your loved ones, pets and property. If not what is left distributed by law so your diamond ring might be gracing the finger of a relative you detest or even worse your property ends up being squandered by the state. Making a will does not have to be a time consuming or expensive proposition. This program walks you through the basics. Installation was easy. You have the option of using a CD or download.  I used the download without issue.The program is basic but adequate. In addition to wills, it also addresses simple trusts as well as documents pertaining to end of life decisions including power of attorney, medical directives and final disposition/funeral arrangements. There is also a free e-book available on special needs trusts. While not the end all and be all of estate planning it is solid and easy to follow.

title: Easy and Intuitive
text: I am new to digital painting and was looking for a program that was easy and intuitive. This one fits the bill. Installation was simple and straightforward. there are excellent training videos to help you learn the program.  while experienced digital artists might feel limited, the program is ideal for beginners. There are plenty of options but not an overwhelming number. it is fun to play with no frustrating glitches or tricks. It will take some time before I master it but it is an entertaining skill to learn. This program is well designed, accessible and interesting. Well done.

title: Comprehensive
text: The number of billable hours generated can make or break a law firm. This software is comprehensive. Installation was simple. After inserting the cd (downloadable version also available) it was just a matter of following the instructions and inputting the included serial number and reference code. Mastery of the program is more time consuming. Even if you have experience with other programs it behooves you to review the guides and videos. The program is not hard but there are many components from tracking time, generating bills, inputting expenses and keeping current on account status. It is logical and the results are professional. This program is as useful for solo practioners and small firms as it is for large ones. It still takes time but covers just about everything. It distills an often unwieldy process into a doable, usable, logical system. Satisfied.

title: Bust Not Boon
text: I spend my days fighting in court; I don't need to fight software in the office. I had previous versions of Dragon, both the regular version and an earlier professional version and wasn't impressed. But I had hoped things had improved. After all, even the robotic customer service calls have gotten a bit more sensitive. Unfortunately, this one is not the timesaver I had hoped. It does not recognize common legal terms easily and some of its interpretations are laughable if they were wasting time and money. Even worse the program crashes frequently and without warning. Even more annoying, it forgets your voice.  If they could polish their program it would be a boon. But as it is, it is a bust.

title: Steep Learning curve
text: Although feature-laden this software has a steep learning curve. I did not find it especially intuitive. Accessing their tutorials does help. but my bigger objection is to the subscription plan. That really doesn't work for me. I sue different progr5ams for home and work. My usage varies from light to moderate but what I want are speed and simplicity.  This program touts its multimedia capabilities and they do seem to be impressive. Before committing determine your needs and whether the subscription model works for you.

title: Gets the Job done
text: This is solid software. It covers the bases. Installation is fairly straightforward and the interface is intuitive. it does have a dated look, not especially attractive but it gets the job done.  Frankly, I am more concerned with ease of input that prettiness. Not hard but probably more than I need for the business I am using it for. Still, it is decent and seems reliable.

title: Solid Accounting software
text: this software was simple to install and easy to use. Despite having many features it is still simple and intuitive. There are a number of versions available and subscriptions. You have to examine your business to see whether the investment is justified. I have used various software over the years for my businesses and have yet to find the perfect fit., that being said simplicity is a major selling point. It is pricey but a solid set of accounting software.

title: Fun but Buggy
text: The game is fun and tests your ability to recognize and put together words. Unfortunately, it is buggy. I have uninstalled and reinstalled three separate times. Probably will not bother if it happens the fourth time. Essentially the game just freezes and nothing gets it going again. too bad since it is otherwise enjoyable.

title: basic Small Biz Software
text: This is basic accounting software that is designed for small business. Had not problems with installation. There is a learning curve, although it probably is not as steep for those with backgrounds in finance or accounting. It is a one user program and obviously has limits on functions. You can pay bills and get paid, organize and manage your finances. Payroll is available for an additional fee. The UI feels dated and is not especially intuitive. It is pricey especially if you add on.

title: Very Good
text: PDF edition and conversion are not my favorite tasks. This software is a step up from Adobe Acrobat. Though installation is slow, the end result is definitely a boost to office productivity. Do read the online manual to get a full understanding. I think it is easy to master and use and have been pleased with its performance.

title: Not Sure How to Train My Dragon
text:
Maybe I don't know how to train my dragon but I find this program inconsistent. Set up was easy enough and the voice recognition works pretty well. Perfect, no, but not bad. this software is designed for professional and includes transcription as well as dictation, command and control. While I was ns't expecting it to accurately capture professional jargon, legal and/or medical terms, the word errors struck me as high. dictation was somewhat better but seems to depend on the application. You can dictate directly into word, excel and notepad. with other apps a dictation box oopens. Your words appear in that and then can be transferred in. However I had real difficulties getting it to work with Outlook and Onenote. Outlook did not respond to the commands to open and I was unable to successfully and consistently transfer dictation into OneNote. I also had the equally annoying problem of having text disappear from the text box. You can perform web searches and use web apps like Wordpress. Again, I had more misses than hits. There are plenty of options to customize by addings words and phrases but the interface is unwieldy.

The feature I was most interested in was transcription. However my results were a muddled mess. It is supposed to be able to transcribe audio files. but the results were inaccurate and in cases with multiple voices almost nonsensical I was hoping to integrate this into my business. However the consistency is not what I need. It does seem to work best with computer control than web searches, etc. and with standard apps like Word and Excel. the voice recognition is pretty good. wWhile my failure to achieve the results and speed I desired may be partially due to my lack of program mastery, the program too is in need of improvement. .

title: Impairs PC Performance
text: this is an adequate security suite. two features distinguish it from the more baxi versions: namely file encryption and a password manager for up to five accounts. Both are nice features to have. It is multi platform and can handle both MAc and PC. However it substantially slowed the performance of my pc. the parental controls seemed clunky. and frankly I did not like that their anti virus guarantee was conditioned on signing up for auto renewal. I don't do aut renewals. In view of how sophisticated hackers are and how slow corporations are in notifying customers of breaches and possible compromises,it is just not for me. You need to input credit card info to install. You can go to the Account tb and turn off the auto renewal but good luck removing the credit card information. Additionally there is no way to opt out of the data collection of the anti virus program. there is no option ofor secure banking or a VPN. Although there are some nice features included this suite is not for me.

title: Comprehensive But Cluttered
text: This software contains pretty much anything an amateur or serious hobbyist could want in video software. there are plenty of effects and tools. there are filters that remedy such things as camera shake, noise and lighting. there are a variety of export options and direct uploading to Facebook and YouTube. It is fast and I've experienced no lag  or crashes. However sometimes less is more.I found the interface overwhelming .There is definitely a learning curve. You need to invest time to master it but it is time well spent. I don't know whether the average home user would regularly use even half the features but a dedicated hobbyist or heavy user will welcome the details and options.

title: Intuitive Interface
text: This video editing program is ideal for beginners and intermediates. The interface is amazing. Intuitive and easy to use it is easy to master. They do have tutorials if you run into a problem. the Plus version includes everything in the basic along with 3D editing, screen capture tools, wide angle lens corrections, pro audio tools and an extra three hundred effects. the templates are outstanding andbut the effects run the gamut. I did notice significant lag when working with the effects. this program provides the user with plenty of contorl and you can really fashion your video the way you want. The best aspect of this program is the interface. I had an earlier version of Pinnacle Studio. Despite the many features it wa virtually unused because of the interface. This improvement insures frequent use and satisfactory output.

title: Easy to Use
text:
what I like best about this version of Pinnacle Studio is the interface. it is clean, modern and easy to use. Earlier versions were a nightmare as it was clunky and outdated but they have remedied those deficiencies. Thoughtfully redesigned, it is fun and intuitive. This software comes with a wide array of effects. My favorites are the templates and the morph transitions. The templates look professional and the morph transitions add a pro touch easily.

However my experience was not completely rosy. The effects, or at least the ones I tried, are slow and the system crashed a couple of times. Still the ease of use makes up for a lot and with the wide variety and easy interface this is a system most beginners and intermediates can master without tremendous effort or frustration. It allows you to create a polished product that almost looks professional.

title: Much Improved Interface
text: I had an earlier version of this suite which I never used due to the complicated and confusing interface and the buggy performance. This version is muched improved. While there still is a fairly steep learning curve to master all the functions, the interface has been redesigned. It is much easier to follow and fairly intuitive. Major improvement. Performance wise it seems that they have erased many of the glitches. Previously I had a big problem with it crashing and freezing. To date I've experienced neither. It has many great features and with the improved interface it is easier to produce and post polisihed videos.

title: Single Device Protection
text: This is a comprehensive program that addresses pretty much all the concerns one has about keeping a computer safe and secure. highly rated by PC magazine it is designed for a single pc. It was easy to download and install. It does require registration and input of the key. My main objection with previous editions of Norton was that it noticeably interfered with computer speed. While this one also makes the computer a tad slower, it is not as bad as earlier editions. I currently am using it on a single desktop. The computer is older and available for guests. It is usually used by my mother who has not great need for speed. But that being said it only slows it down by a second or two so it is not a big deal. Ia m not a fan of auto billing but we will cross that bridge when we come to it. If you are looking for single device proitection, this certainly provides a broad range of coverage.

title: full of fun Effects
text: With the right software anyone can produce polished and professional movies. The Ultimate version of cyberlink Power Director 15 has more features than the average person will ever use. That being said it is impressive. While the 360 video editing is very cool and the vertical video probably the most practical, I was most impressed by video blending. the program itself was easy to install However if you are not familiar with photo editing software it behooves you to check out the tutorials before you being playing around. Had I not done so, I probably would have been frustrated by the experience. However, overall it is a decent product that encourages creativty. The color quality is amazing and the choice of effects is impressive.

title: Great for Phone Footage
text: My favorite feature of this software is the vertical video. I'be been using my phone more frequently for video. Neither the standard no widescreen aspect ratios work well with phone footage. This feature makes it easy to upload, edit and publish that footage. It is designed to work with the 9:16 vertical video format. This software is feature laden and has fun effects and design elements. If you are accustomed to this type of video editing software,it is fairly easy to use. There is a bit of a learning curve for complete novices. It has an easy mode that is perfect for beginners. Set up is simple and I've encountered no problems like freezing. It is a comprehensive program that helps produce pretty impressive videos.

title: Some Very Cool Effects
text:
You've got to love software that helps you look slim and trim with a few clicks. This software has some truly nifty features including the multiple exposure effect, video to photo, blur tools, color splash and my favorite the beautifier. The Beautifier is essentially plastic surgery without the pain. It includes tols to improve the body, face and skin.It removes wrinkles, eye bags, dark circles and contours the face, tightening jowls and contouring features while evening skin tone. don't like the improvements? Easy enough to redo. You can tag the faces and share directly to Facebook and flickr.

This is an attractive program that is fun to use. The interface is clear and if is easy to use if you ave experience using photo workflow/editing software. It contains some super cool effects that most definitely up your game.

title: Good for Mobile Office
text: In many ways my car functions as mobile office. This software is easy to install and performs well. I frequently need documents and these pdfs are easy to edit and individualize. It eliminates the delay of having to either go back to my main office or go back and forth via phone, email and fax. Just pull up, fill in and print, can't be easier than that. I am not a fan of subscriptions and like the disc. the output looks good and it is fairly intuitive.

title: Good But Not Ideal For On-line Enterprises
text: This software provides pretty much everything a small business needs. It is easy to master and understand and is fairly attractive. I do like that it is a single disc that you own. However I was annoyed by a couple of things. First they for pay roll add ons and they  don't interface with services such as PayPal or with online banking. If you don't do a lot of transactions on line then this software is a comprehensive and easy to use solution. But even brick and mortar businesses are doing more and more on-line and it seems that Sage would want to accommodate that need. Factor in the charges for cloud storage and this becomes far less attractive. I was hoping to use this primarily for an on-line business but it doesn't seem to be designed for that. Still it is decent software that is reliable and works well for the right business.

title: Good Protection
text: Finally, a security product that doesn't slow down your computer.  I installed this on my new Lenovo laptop and have been very pleased. It downloaded without issue and appears to be working well. While I prefer CDs to key cards, I must say it was hassle free. this suite will accommodate up to three devices. the packaging was excessive for just a little piece of paper but other than that I have no complaints. This product provides reliable protection without bogging down your device or cluttering it with unwanted extras.

title: It's Okay
text: Although I prefer to install from a disk, this product installed without issue. the promised protection is comprehensive though at this point it is hard to gauge just how efficient it is. I do think it slows down the computer. I installed on a desktop that had a free version of avira. This program is a tad more intuitive when it comes to scheduling scans but there has been a marked decrease in speed and responsiveness. I will probably uninstall this since the slowness is really annoying. So it is okay if you don't have a need for speed.

title: Let The Music Move You
text:
This software is fun although it does hit a few sour notes. Set up is slow but once installed it is attractive. Feature laden, it provides plenty of options to creat music. as they say little knowledge is a dangerous thing. I am new when it comes to this type of software so I did take the time to watch their tutorials and found them helpful. Once you get going, it is addictive and fun. the drag and drop feature is cool and easy to use. I can't say my results were professional lbut they were acceptable for youtube videos and the like.

the biggest problem I encountered was the crashing. Windows 10 may be responsible but it is annoying. They include an optional registry cleaner. Be sure to watch for it. Inclusion of that type of product is one of my pet peeves. Once installed, they can be a hassle to remove. But if you are novice, this program is worth considering. Once you be accustomed to it, it is simple to let the music move you. 3.5 stars

title: Adequate
text: This software promises to deliver cutting edge protection to up to three devices. a key card is enclosed in a box. You must log on to the Norton website, input the code and download. It is easy enough. It appears to be working although you never know unless there is a problem. I can't say that there is significantly more protection than you get with other products. That being said, it seems to be rather silly and wasteful to send a single keycard in a box. I generally do not do auto renewals and felt that this company pressures customers to do so. I've used earlier versions and found them satisfactory and expect this one to perform as well.

title: Polish Your Production
text: With the right program, even an amateur can create a polished production. This software offers a generous menu of features including live screen capture and auto ducking. I had no problems loading it but found it slow at times. Free training is available for thirty days. It is worthwhile, especially for amateurs and I do wish it was free for the life of the software. Although I am far from mastering all of its features, it does have plenty to inspire. It is a good program for folks with a working knowledge of editing software.

title: Comprehensive But...
text:
This accounting soft ware is comprehensive with much to offer. I selected it for my business but it is not compatible with Windows Ten. So I took it home with the intent to use for a small and as of yet not profitable business venture and household accounting. It installed seamlessly on the Windows 7 computer. The software itself is attractive. I especially liked the invoicing by email. It is very helpful if you must manage inventory. that being said, it is not for novices and it does not do payroll. While the company offers payroll and payment processing compatible with this suite it requires subscription to one of their subscription services. They do have a tiered schedule of service plans ranging from Basic where you own the software and receive a year of free updates to and auto subscribe monthly service. The Basic plan cannot be used with payroll.
Before committing to this or for that matter any accounting software, it is wise to carefully assess your needs and try a free trial. I am working my way through the program and learning a lot. However, it is more than I actually need , If your accounting skills are intermediate to advanced and you manage significant inventory and are interested in strategic planning and metrics, this program would most likely satisfy you. But for my business needs at this time, less is more.

title: Unimpressive
text: I was unimpressed by this product. While it did contain a fairly good variety of forms, you could cull the same or some of superior quality from other (free) sources online. They are essentially general templates. It is a starting point but not much more. While installation was easy, the look is dated. Included is a little book that contains plenty of interesting information, although not exclusively devoted to legal and business concerns. In short, this will not save you much time in doing research and personalization and if it is important enough, you should consult an attorney.

title: Basic But Good
text:
Electronic devices are like bunnies. Every time I turn around there are more of them. McAfee LiveSafe is a commendable program. It may be basic but it is good. First off I like that you get protection for an unlimited number of devices. The program is downloaded and installed easily. I used it on two android phones and a guest computer. It operates without interfering with performance or speed. The protection is adequate although for my work computers I need more comprehensive protection.

If you are looking for affordable, basic protection for multiple devices, this product is definitely worth considering. It is affordable, effective, easy to install and use and does not slow you down. It is basic but good. Recommended.

title: Can't Beat the One Click Fix
text:
This collection of mainentence software utilities is quick, easy and seemingly effective. Easy to install, it detects preexisting virus protection and gives you the option to use it or theirs. The utilities are divided into three separate catgfories: Security, Optimise and Fix and Maintain. Security lets you set up different types of scans and set up block list. Optimize has a fast disk defrag tool among others. Fix and Maintain deals with system restore and file recovery and a scheduler for automatic maintenence. The company also provided a rather thick owner's manual which I appreciated.

I don't know that the scans are quicker than my previous system but the computer is running significantly faster. The One Click Fix is sufficient for the average user but I like that there are options for those more technically adept. The cost is reasonable and the scope is comprehensive. Recommended.

title: Failed Potential
text:
I'm really torn about this app. We downloaded it several months ago and my 4 year old loves it. However, while perusing the Lego website we saw that an update which includes the new Arctic Expedition line was available. I was unable to update the app via my Kindle Fire, but since my child was so enthusiastic about the update I deleted and reinstalled it several times in an effort to get the updated version (via the web browser- the game no longer comes up from my tablet store). None of my attempts worked, the old version reinstalled each time. (Please note that, at this time, the app states that the Arctic update is included and that you can synchronize to your Lego account- I've found neither of these to be true.)

So while I have a preschooler who adores this game, redundancy and all, I also have a preschooler who is disappointed that he is unable to play with the updates. And since we were never allowed to synchronize the app to our Lego account, all of the progress he made (to the tune of 17k studs) was lost.

For what this game is, and to a target audience of preschoolers, this game is great. The repetitive nature of the mini-games appeal to that age group, no matter how I personally wince when it is my turn to rescue the minifigs from the ocean again. However, with the issues I've had trying to update it, I have to assume that this is no longer supported for Kindle which means that, as a Kindle app, the cons outweigh the pros for me. It has potential, but it fails to meet it.

title: Enjoy it - had a problem - soon to be resolved!
text:
I really like the game play. Went through all the levels. Challenging was, in fact really hard for me - but I liked it a lot. It was very frustrating, because I like to finish without using any errors or hints. I ended up restarting the Challenging levels a lot - some times, due to my error, others times due to the selection of hexes when just moving around the board.

The problem that I experienced was that the game would freeze, on my Kindle HDX. Had to restart MANY times. Tried to remember to save the puzzles every couple of moves. I tried the Zoo one, on that Kindle, and it seemed to work fine. I ended up finishing the Challenging levels on my original Kindle. Now I know why I kept my old Kindle ;)

I will be picking up more of these games.

Update: Developer was immediately on this. He has contacted me (see comments) to try to resolve. Thinks he did, and has a work around. Turn off sounds, and it shouldn't hang. I have been trying with sound off, and didn't replicate the problem.

Giving five stars, because that is what I call service...

title: There is an excellent font selection on this version which I appreciate
text: I had used Print Master in the past on my Windows Vista and when that computer crashed I discovered that my old version would not load on the newer Windows 8 environment.  I read the reviews and it appeared that this would work on Windows 7 and 8 so I gave it a try.  It did load quickly on both my Windows 7 and 8 computers, and seems to function as it should.  A few things are in different locations than they were in the past, but overall the program is the same.  Many of the graphics require the install disc to be in the driver, or require downloading from on-line, but they do load properly which is an improvement over earlier versions which never seemed to load correctly.  Have used it many times and there have been no performance problems in Windows 7 or 8.  There is an excellent font selection on this version which I appreciate.  Overall, it appears to be working just fine.

title: Simpe but addictive
text: Again, another one of those games for the grandkids in the back seat of the car and now a Grandma game too!  I got hooked when I tried it out after the download...never expected that.  The object of the game is to connect the dots of matching color without crossing over the lines connecting other dots.  Simple concept, but it keeps you thinking.  The game is simple enough for the younger children but if you up the square count it becomes more difficult to move about the cube without crossing over yourself.  It can be played with a timer or without and another one of the games where YOU DO NOT NEED to be on line to use the game - a real plus.  Bottom line, great game for the money, clear graphics and lots of fun.

title: This one is fun!
text: I downloaded a number of games on the Kindle for the grandkids when we are in the car together.  I had no real knowledge of the game or how it was played, but it sounded interesting.  Well it surprised me because when I sat down to look at it after the download I got hooked!  It is simple enough for the younger children but still interesting enough to keep you sitting and playing with them.  The other part I REALLY LIKED was that you do not have to be on line to use the game, that is a real plus.  I downloaded several of the other hidden objects games and they all worked well.  Graphics were really clear and you can turn the sound off if it makes you crazy!  Overall, very well pleased on all aspects of this purchase and we have peace in the back seat!

title: like photoshop
text:
I have Photoshop Elements and would love to have regular Photoshop but refuse to pay a monthly fee for something I may use every other month. I don't need the latest and greatest as picture taking and graphic arts are just sometime hobbies for me. So I am always looking for something to replace it.

Cyberink PhotoDirector seems to be a good replacement. It is very similar in that it has the layers and wizards. For me it is a little complicated, but I am figuring it out slowly. As I said, I don't use it much and my favorite photo editor program doesn't technically work with Windows 10. I will definitely be playing with it more, as I haven't figured out how to do all the photo fixing yet in it. I also haven't figured out if you can do the graphics stuff that you can do with Photoshop or if you are limited to fixing photos only without paying for extra packs.

The only really bad thing was that you need to keep a backup copy when you download it. Although it came with a disc, the actually program is downloaded, you don't have an account, so once you download it, you may not be able to get another copy of the download. This could be a problem if your computer dies, or in my case, gets stolen. Fortunately, I did have a copy in my backup.

title: Buggy - Buy at Your Own Risk!!!
text:
Played half the game, paid to unlock, then game will not open. Keeps looping through opening screens, but will not stop. Deleting and re- downloading does not help. Big ripoff.

Update - emailed Big Fish - they sent a bunch of trouble shooting tips all related to the Kindle. Will not accept responsibility for this buggy software. This has been reported to Amazon. Hopefully they will pull this game from their app store.

Update - This morning, I tried it again, and what do you know? It works. I have now completed the main game, and have started the bonus game. Didn't change anything on my Kindle, so I don't know how it is working. Will update my rating slightly once I am able to complete the entire game.

Update - Was able to complete both main game and bonus game, plus all extras. I have changed to a 3 star rating based on the fact that the graphics and music are awesome. The game play is inventive and there is a nice variety on the puzzles and HOGs. I really did enjoy playing this game. I just wish I didn't have to go through all of the hassle with Big Fish when it suddenly stopped working. I have no idea why that happened, or what fixed it, which is very disturbing.

title: Not Worth Full Price At All
text: I don't understand how anyone could think this game was long. I finished it in a couple of hours. And there is no bonus game - it's part of the main game, so that makes it even shorter!  The HO scenes are very boring; luckily there is a choice of playing mahjong instead, but those are boring too.  Story is very disjointed, and the graphics are really only so-so compared to some of the better games out there.  I guess it would have been okay if it weren't full price ($4.99) to unlock.  Definitely feel cheated with this one.

title: Love These Games
text: I love Lone Wolf Games.  The graphics are a bit amateurish, but the games are first rate.  Lots of puzzles to solve, and no HOGs.  The bit drawback, and reason for the 4 star rating, is that you cannot jump to scenes from the map, which means LOTS of moving around and around and around.  Still, I loved the game.

title: Pretty Good
text: Really beautiful, detailed graphics.  HO scenes were not very challenging or interesting.  Fair story line.  Good fun.

title: WONDERFUL!
text: Wow, this game has it all.  Never played the first NYMysteries, but will definitely play it now.  First of all, the graphics are spectacular, and the cut scenes are great too.  Lots and lots of stuff to figure out, and everything you do has some purpose appropriate to the story line.  Even the HO scenes makes sense - no fishing around for items that have no meaning or purpose.  I love this game.  It did crash a few times on my Kindle 7, but  my progress was saved anyway.  Highly recommend this one.

title: WHY IS THERE NO TEN STAR RATING FOR THIS GAME?????
text: OMG, I didn't think it could be better than the first one, but IT IS!!!!  Once again, Fireproof Games has given us a sophisticated, artistic, and challenging experience, immersing us in a world of puzzles and magic.  Just like The Room, the art is spectacular, the music and sound effects rich and full, and the whole experience so fully satisfying that my only complaint is that I finished it.  Quite a bit more challenging than your run-of-the-mill puzzle game, but there is also a wonderful hint system that progressively gives you just enough help without giving everything away.  Oh PLEASE, Fireproof Games, make more of these!  Until then, I will just have to play these two OVER and OVER again!  Thank you so much for this game development that is so far above the rest out there, it's just not possible to praise it enough.

title: NOT LIKE ANY OTHER GAME YOU HAVE PLAYED!!!
text: This game is truly a work of art.  If you are tired of silly adventure and escape games, this game is for you.  Step inside a world of magical objects and secret compartments.  Wonder at the beautiful detailed graphics, listen to the haunting music and sound effects.  Challenge yourself to figure out the puzzles, and play in a way that no other game lets you.  Tilt your tablet to move an orb, use two fingers to manipulate objects, this game just doesn't stop delivering innovative techniques.  Love, love, love this game.  Can't wait to try the next one.  Keep it up, Fireproof Games.  Awesome work!

title: Decent product, it just works for me.  I'd recommend it 'at the right price.'  shop smartly.
text:
I have a love/hate relationship with this product. I love it for it's technical ability to provide antivirus, optimizations and other things. I hate it for the way that Symantec plays games with it's price.

The whole auto-renewal thing (which I always turn off) to renew at some insanely high price is bonkers. You could just wait a while and shop smartly or even hold out until a megasale (like Black Friday, etc.) and get it at a fraction of the renewal cost. I think that if they actually want sane users to use the auto-renewal feature they should bring the cost more inline with what you'd see at a sales price. It doesn't have to be the absolute low but at least a level where it doesn't make me feel that I'm being totally taken advantage of to use a convenience feature like "auto-renewal".

Bottom line, decent product, it just works for me. I'd recommend it 'at the right price.' shop smartly.

title: Echo...Train it like a puppy.
text:
The Echo is very useful. I use it all the time for music, weather and news. I use To Do list and Shopping List skills all the time. I love being able to call out and get something on a list as soon as I realize I need it.

When I cook I can ask for food substitutions, get help cutting reciepies in half and set timers without touching the stove with my hands.

I will say it took a few weeks to decide to keep Echo. When I first got it, I tried to add too many skills at once. Then I got frustrated because I could not remember the syntax for all the skills. Plus Echo will still learning my voice.

I pulled back and treated it like a new puppy. We learned one or two skills at a time and everything came together and I am looking forward to implementing some smart home devices.

title: More of a basic yoga class than a training DVD
text:
I came across this DVD recently at a used book sale. I was excited to find a yoga training DVD since I am a yoga teacher. I wasn't trained in the YogaFit model, but I thought this DVD would offer a good refresher.

Unfortunately, this isn't really a training DVD at all. Instead, it is a full yoga class taught by YogaFit instructors--that might appeal to some, but it wasn't what I wanted or expected. The DVD offers only two short segments that have any kind of "training" information. Here is the breakdown from the main menu (times added by me):

The Full Workout (Play All)
1. Introduction/Transformation Language (2:14)
2. Mountain One - Standing Warm-Up (12:21)
3. Sun Salutations (7:11)
4. Mountain Two - Work (22:39)
5. Mountain Three - Cool Down (15:12)
Bonus Features:
Mountain One - Supine Warm-Up (16:18)
SPA Principles (7:04)
Essence of YogaFit (brief)

The SPA refers to the Seven Principles of Alignment. These are reviewed very briefly as follows: 1) establishing base and creating dynamic tension, 2) creating core stability, 3) aligning the spine, 4) softening and aligning the knees, 5) relaxing the shoulders back and down, 6) hinging at the hips, and 7) shortening the lever.

The class itself is a basic beginning-level yoga class. I found it a bit boring and uninspired--it seemed to stick to a pattern of dynamic movements, always repeated exactly 10 times. I also didn't really like any of the instructors; none seemed genuine to me, as they all spoke in an affected tone which I tend to think of as "fake yoga teacher voice." Finally, the production values on this DVD are poor, including unattractive lighting and annoying music. So although I enjoy a variety of yoga styles, this practice just wasn't for me.

This DVD might be better suited to someone already familiar with YogaFit and looking for a home practice in that style.

title: fun but slow
text: Fun game, very much a straightforward gem game, with essentially no story or characters (the game does allow you to bypass them easily, and since there's never any point in interacting with the pirates you run into, I always skip those scenes). I do prefer games with good graphics, and my feelings here are mixed: the backgrounds are bad cartoons, but the gems are reasonably attractive. The directions for using the extra power items are poor. They're only explained once, and only graphically at that, and there's no way to look them up if you forget how they work. My main issue, however, is how slow it is to load between games--you waste a lot of time staring at the ugly landscape. I'm also not clear how it scores. Games I thought should receive more extra points don't get them, and vice versa. The difficulty of the individual games is also not linear. Easy are intermixed with hard. There are lot of levels--I'm in the 30s of 450 in the first of three boards.

2. The app is too slow to say I've already pinned a photo, so I double-pin it before I see the warning.

title: a fun way to spend time, but limited
text: 3.5* I like both Bonza games. They can be educational and I like word and puzzle games. I do wish they'd change how they handle levels of difficulty, though. Right now, each puzzle pack begins with very easy (sometimes stupidly easy) puzzles usually involving 3 - 4 words, then moves on to puzzles with more words. These tend to be harder, though not in any very organized fashion. I wish they would arrange the puzzles so there were packs of just harder ones, as well as packs that start easy and get more difficult. I also wish there were puzzles that were even more difficult than the ones they offer, as I find them mostly quite easy. The jigsaw puzzles also aren't difficult, so maybe making those so you can choose the number of pieces would help.

text: Having played this game for a few months, I would never recommend it. A. it cheats you on elapsed time.B. you have to constantly buy energy or gems or coins in order to keep playing. C. It's constantly crashing or freezing on my Kindle-- generally just after I've used potions, of course. D. The potions are a joke. They allegedly offer you benefits, but I haven't noticed any difference with or without--that is especially frustrating if you've paid cash for extended energy potions and your money goes to waste. E. There's no bemefit for playing well. I don't see the point of randomly generated prizes rather than more/better prizes for better play. F. It's impossible to get some of the needed objects (as they seem to be offered on some kind of timer) so you can turn in the collections that offer useful prizes, while easily amassing dozens of other useless things. Secret Society, by the same people, is exactly the same sort of rip-off.
